speaker: Unknown
en: Most adults in the corporate environment are really just babies in disguise.
zh: 企业环境中的大多数成年人实际上只是伪装的婴儿。

speaker: Unknown
en: All humans need the same things, whether we're one or five or 45 or 85.
zh: 所有人类都需要同样的东西，无论我们是一岁、五岁、四十五岁还是八十五岁。

speaker: Unknown
en: When you look at bad behavior, the actual problem is someone doesn't have the skill they need to manage something happening internally.
zh: 当你观察不良行为时，真正的问题是某人缺乏管理其内部发生的事情所需的技能。

speaker: Unknown
en: Love your advice.
zh: 喜欢你的建议。

speaker: Unknown
en: It works not just for kids, it works for adults.
zh: 它不仅对孩子有效，对成年人也有效。

speaker: Unknown
en: Our whole parenting philosophy is resilience over happiness.
zh: 我们整个育儿理念是抗压能力优于幸福。

speaker: Unknown
en: When we're thinking about a resilient work culture, we want people who can say, "This is hard and I can do hard things."
zh: 当我们考虑有韧性的工作文化时，我们希望人们能说，“这很难，但我能做困难的事。”

speaker: Unknown
en: You teach kids are good inside, no matter their behavior, is that useful in work?
zh: 你教导孩子无论行为如何内心是好的，这在工作中是否有用？

speaker: Unknown
en: The idea of being good inside inherently requires us to separate behavior and identity.
zh: “内心是好的”这一理念本质上要求我们将行为和身份区分开来。

speaker: Unknown
en: We infer a lot from people's behavior.
zh: 我们从人们的行为中推断出很多东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Someone's late to work a lot, "Oh, that person's lazy."
zh: 某人上班经常迟到，“噢，那个人很懒。”

speaker: Unknown
en: The quickest way to have an unproductive conversation is to lose sight of the fact that someone's good inside.
zh: 进行一次无成效对话的最快方式，就是无视某人内心是好的这一事实。

speaker: Unknown
en: I definitely wanted to ask you about this idea of boundaries.
zh: 我绝对想问问你关于边界这个想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: Boundaries are what you tell someone else you will do, and it requires the other person to do nothing.
zh: 边界是你告诉别人你要做什么，这不需要对方做任何事。

speaker: Unknown
en: Making a request, that's not a boundary.
zh: 提出请求，不是边界。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is there a corollary to adult work environments in potty learning?
zh: 在成人工作环境中是否有与如厕训练相对应的事物？

speaker: Unknown
en: Today, my guest is Dr. Becky Kennedy, a clinical psychologist, author, and CEO of one of the most popular parenting books, podcasts, communities, and apps called "Good Inside."
zh: 今天，我的嘉宾是 Dr. Becky Kennedy，一位临床心理学家、作家，也是最受欢迎的育儿书籍、播客、社区和应用程序“Good Inside”的 CEO。

speaker: Unknown
en: Why would I have a parenting expert on this podcast?
zh: 我为什么要在播客上请育儿专家？

speaker: Unknown
en: Because if you think about it, many of the people that we work with in the workplace act a lot more like babies than adults.
zh: 因为如果你想想，我们在工作中共事的许多人，其行为表现更像婴儿而不是成年人。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'm half joking, but I'm half not.
zh: 我是半开玩笑，但我也是半不开玩笑。

speaker: Unknown
en: Think about coworkers that got really mad when they had to share their stuff, that always need to be the center of attention, that get really upset about not getting their way, that need other people to fix things for them.
zh: 想想那些因为必须分享东西而非常生气的同事，那些总是需要成为关注中心的同事，那些因为没有如愿以偿而非常心烦的同事，那些需要别人为他们解决问题的同事。

speaker: Unknown
en: These are just a few examples, and there is a lot that we can learn about how to effectively deal with people at work from a parenting expert like Dr. Becky.
zh: 这只是几个例子，我们可以从像 Dr. Becky 这样的育儿专家那里学到很多关于如何有效应对工作中的人的知识。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have never heard a conversation like this anywhere that bridges the gap between parenting advice and leadership advice, and you will leave this conversation both a better leader and a better parent.
zh: 我在任何地方都从未听说过这样的对话，它架起了育儿建议和领导力建议之间的桥梁，你将在这次对话中离开时成为更好的领导者和更好的父母。

speaker: Unknown
en: We talk about the power of repair, the importance of building long-term resilience versus short-term happiness.
zh: 我们谈论了修复的力量，建立长期抗压能力与短期幸福感的重要性。

speaker: Unknown
en: Why your goal as a leader is to become sturdy, the power of curiosity over judgment, the framework Dr. Becky calls the most generous interpretation, also a ton of really specific phrases that work really well with kids and adults who you're having a hard time with.

speaker: Unknown

speaker: Unknown

en: Don't let your competitors out hire you.

en: Metaview customers close roles 30% faster.

en: Try Metaview today for free and get an extra month of sourcing at metaview.ai/lenny.

en: That's Metaview.ai/lenny.

speaker: Unknown
en: Dr. Becky, thank you so much for being here and welcome to the podcast.
zh: Becky 博士，非常感谢你的到来，欢迎来到播客。

speaker: Unknown
en: I am truly very excited to be here.
zh: 我真的很兴奋能来到这里。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm an avid listener of your podcast and all of your content, so I'm honored to be here.
zh: 我是你播客和你所有内容的忠实听众，所以我很荣幸能来到这里。

speaker: Unknown
en: I can say exactly the same thing.
zh: 我也可以说完全一样的话。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm even more excited.
zh: 我甚至更兴奋。

speaker: Unknown
en: First of all, just what is Dr. Becky doing on this podcast?
zh: 首先，Becky 博士究竟在这个播客上做什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's help people understand what's going on here.
zh: 让我们帮助人们理解这里发生了什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have an explanation, but I want to ask you first, actually.
zh: 我有一个解释，但我想先问问你，实际上。

speaker: Unknown
en: What's the idea here?
zh: 这里的想法是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: I think Good Inside, which is kind of I guess the company and the method around all the things I've thought about is known for helping parents with parenting struggles and different things that are going on with our kids at various ages.
zh: 我认为 Good Inside，我想这就是公司以及围绕我所思考的所有事情的方法，以帮助父母解决育儿困扰和不同年龄段孩子身上发生的各种事情而闻名。

speaker: Unknown
en: But at the end of the day, it's a set of core principles that help us better understand human beings, ourself, the core relationships we're in, why we do the things we do and why we act the way we do in certain systems.
zh: 但归根结底，这是一套核心原则，帮助我们更好地理解人类，理解我们自己，理解我们所处的核心关系，理解我们为什么做我们所做的事，以及为什么我们在某些系统中会这样行动。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the system I tend to focus on the most is the family system.
zh: 而我最倾向于关注的系统是家庭系统。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the workplace is another system, a marriage is a system, sibling relationships are a system, extended family over the holidays, that's a system.
zh: 但职场是另一个系统，婚姻是一个系统，兄弟姐妹关系是一个系统，节日期间的大家庭也是一个系统。

speaker: Unknown
en: And once you start to think through a lens of understanding how we operate in the system, any good principle can then be applied to any system.
zh: 一旦你开始透过一个理解我们如何在系统中运作的镜头来思考，任何好的原则就可以应用到任何系统中。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'm very oriented around efficiency also.
zh: 我也非常注重效率。

speaker: Unknown
en: So to me, what Good Inside really gives people is a way to think about themselves and the way to think about leadership.
zh: 所以对我来说，Good Inside 真正给人们的是一种思考自己的方式，以及一种思考领导力的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: And whether we're talking about leadership in parenting or leadership in the workplace, it's actually the exact same principles that can be applied.
zh: 无论我们谈论的是育儿中的领导力还是职场中的领导力，其实都可以应用完全相同的原则。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the good news for that is whether you're a parent, you're a leader at work, you're both, learning one system and applying it to multiple areas becomes a very efficient way to think about showing up in our relationships in a way that feels better to everyone.
zh: 对此的好消息是，无论你是父母，你是工作中的领导者，还是两者兼而有之，学习一个系统并将其应用到多个领域，成为了一种非常高效的思考方式，让我们以一种让每个人都感觉更好的方式出现在我们的关系中。

speaker: Unknown
en: Awesome.
zh: 太棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love that explanation.
zh: 我喜欢这个解释。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have another lens that's-
zh: 我有另一个视角，它可能——

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, okay.
zh: 噢，好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: ... maybe funny, maybe too real.
zh: ……也许很有趣，也许太真实了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I have a former podcast guest, Shreyas Doshi.
zh: 所以我有一位前播客嘉宾，Shreyas Doshi。

speaker: Unknown
en: He's one of my favorite product thinkers.
zh: 他是我最喜欢的产品思想家之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: He's been on the podcast a couple times.
zh: 他做过几次播客。

speaker: Unknown
en: He's got this brand that most adults in the corporate environment are really just babies in disguise.
zh: 他有这样一个观点，即企业环境中的大多数成年人实际上只是伪装成婴儿。

speaker: Unknown
en: They look like adults, they act like adults, but they're really babies.
zh: 他们看起来像成年人，行事像成年人，但他们真的是婴儿。

speaker: Unknown
en: For example, they want to always be the center of attention.
zh: 比如，他们总是想成为关注的焦点。

speaker: Unknown
en: They don't want to share stuff they've accumulated.
zh: 他们不想分享他们积累的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: They want to keep their resources, their budget, their people.
zh: 他们想保留他们的资源、预算、人员。

speaker: Unknown
en: They get irrationally upset about things not going their way.
zh: 他们对事情不顺心感到非理性的沮丧。

speaker: Unknown
en: Even when it's just a terrible idea, they're just like, "Nah, that was my idea.
zh: 即使这只是一个糟糕的主意，他们只是说，“不，那是我的主意。

speaker: Unknown
en: I wanted this thing to happen."
zh: 我想要这件事发生。”

speaker: Unknown
en: There's also just power struggles and, "No, no, I want this to happen because it's what I want."
zh: 还有权力斗争，以及，“不，不，我想要这发生，因为这是我想要的。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And they often just need people to fix things for them.
zh: 而且他们通常只是需要别人为他们解决问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: For example, today my toddler, he just grabbed this Yoto player that we have and he threw it across the room.
zh: 比如，今天我的幼儿，他只是抓起我们有的这个 Yoto 播放器，然后把它扔到了房间对面。

speaker: Unknown
en: He's like, " Pick it up."
zh: 他就像说，“把它捡起来。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So with that, I think understanding how babies think and toddlers think and operate is helpful in the corporate environment where we think we're working adults, but a lot of times they're more like babies.
zh: 所以有了这个，我认为理解婴儿和幼儿如何思考和运作，在企业环境中是有帮助的，在这个环境中我们以为我们在和成年人一起工作，但很多时候他们更像婴儿。

speaker: Unknown
en: How does that resonate?
zh: 这如何引起共鸣？

speaker: Unknown
en: I guess my most generous interpretation of what you just said is slightly different but similar, which is that all humans need the same things.
zh: 我想我对您刚才所说的话最宽容的解释略有不同但相似，也就是所有人类都需要同样的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Whether we're one or five or 45 or 85, we tend to need the same things.
zh: 无论我们是一岁、五岁、45 岁还是 85 岁，我们往往需要同样的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: When the needs are not being met, we all tend to express ourselves in kind of ineffective, less than ideal ways.
zh: 当需求没有得到满足时，我们往往都会以一种无效的、不太理想的方式表达自己。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so maybe that's my interpretation of agreeing with what you're saying.
zh: 所以也许这就是我对同意你所说的话的解释。

speaker: Unknown
en: Awesome.
zh: 太棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So let's get into the first topic lesson.
zh: 那么让我们进入第一个课题课程。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's talk about the power of repair, one of your more important and impactful lessons that you teach parents.
zh: 让我们谈谈修复的力量，这是你教给父母的最重要的、最具影响力的课程之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: Explain the toddler, kids version of this and how this is useful in raising kids and then just how this might translate into a corporate environment.
zh: 解释一下针对幼儿和孩子的版本，以及这在抚养孩子方面有何用处，然后再谈谈这可能会如何转化到企业环境中。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think repair is kind of the number one relationship strategy we have.
zh: 我认为修复是我们拥有的头号关系策略。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the thing that keeps us from repairing, which really is the idea of going back to a person after a moment we didn't feel proud of, taking responsibility for our part, maybe acknowledging the impact it had on them and talking about what you would do differently the next time, is actually this very false idea that there's a goal to be perfect.
zh: 而阻碍我们进行修复的——也就是在一个我们感觉不太好的时刻之后回到一个人身边，承担我们自己的责任，也许承认对他们产生的影响，并讨论下次你会采取的不同做法——实际上是这种非常错误的观念，即目标是变得完美。

speaker: Unknown
en: Right?
zh: 对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: And in our family, we say something, we actually now say it at work, "Perfect is creepy."
zh: 在我们的家庭里，我们会说一些话，实际上我们现在在工作中也会说，“完美是令人毛骨悚然的。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I just think it encapsulates... You don't even want to be perfect.
zh: 我只是觉得它概括了……你甚至不想变得完美。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's actually very creepy.
zh: 那实际上是非常令人毛骨悚然的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Only non-humans can ever be perfect.
zh: 只有人类以外的事物才能变得完美。 [[118] 而定义人类状况的是，我们想要做得好，却又一次又一次地搞砸。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what defines kind of the human condition is that we want to do well and we mess up over and over again.
zh: 所谓人类境况的定义在于我们想要做好，但我们却一次又一次地搞砸了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And one of the things I remember learning in clinical psych grad school was that the thing that really differentiated secure attachment, which is the nature of a relationship you really want to have with your kid is the presence of repair.
zh: 我记得在临床心理学研究生院学到的一件事是，真正区分安全依恋（这是你真正想与你的孩子建立的关系的本质）的东西是修复的存在。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I remember the professor continuing to talk and I was kind of stopped in my tracks thinking, "What?
zh: 我记得教授继续讲下去，我有点惊呆了，心想，“什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, so secure attachment isn't defined by getting it right all the time?
zh: 哦，所以安全依恋并不是由一直做对来定义的？

speaker: Unknown
en: Secure attachment is just defined by, we're all going to mess up, but secure attachment has an adult who's willing to repair?"
zh: 安全依恋仅仅是由，我们都会搞砸，但安全依恋中有一个成年人愿意修复？”

speaker: Unknown
en: That felt very hopeful to me because it felt like something I could realistically get good at.
zh: 这让我感到非常充满希望，因为它感觉像是我可以现实地擅长的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And over and over, whether it's with your kid, when you say to them, "Hey, sorry I yelled."
zh: 一次又一次，无论是与你的孩子，当你对他们说，“嘿，抱歉我吼了你。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Some version of, "I had a stressful day at work.
zh: 某种版本的，“我在工作了一天压力很大。

speaker: Unknown
en: That wasn't your fault and I'm working on staying calmer even when I'm upset."
zh: 那不是你的错，我正在努力保持冷静，即使我很心烦。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Or we say to someone, I don't know, on our team, "Hey, earlier in the meeting, I totally cut you off.
zh: 或者我们对某人说，我不知道，在我们的团队里，“嘿，早些时候在会议上，我完全打断了你。

speaker: Unknown
en: I used a really harsh tone.
zh: 我使用了一种非常严厉的语气。

speaker: Unknown
en: Honestly, I did disagree with what you're saying, but it's no excuse for me to talk to you the way I did.
zh: 老实说，我确实不同意你说的，但这不能成为我像那样对你说话的借口。

speaker: Unknown
en: Stuff was going on before the meeting.
zh: 会议之前发生了一些事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm sorry.
zh: 我很抱歉。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm going to work on that."
zh: 我会努力改正的。”

speaker: Unknown
en: There's just nothing to reestablish trust and connection like repair.
zh: 没有任何东西能像修复那样重新建立信任和连接。

speaker: Unknown
en: And when trust and connection are reestablished, then whether it's your kid or someone at work, they cooperate better, you don't get into conversations that are kind of run on conversations that are really an act of defensiveness and you can just get a lot more done.
zh: 当信任和连接重新建立时，无论是你的孩子还是工作中的某个人，他们都会更好地合作，你不会陷入那种冗长的对话，那些对话实际上是一种防御行为，你可以完成更多的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's kind of an associated concept you talk about, which is around connecting before correcting, which reminds me of radical candor a little bit of just this idea of challenge directly but care deeply.
zh: 你谈到的还有一个相关的概念，是关于在纠正之前先连接，这让我想起了“彻底坦诚”，有点像直接挑战但深切关怀的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: Talk a bit about this concept of connecting before correcting.
zh: 谈谈这个在纠正之前先连接的概念。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, I'll use kind of... let's take it out of parenting and work because I think it really shows the importance of connecting in all of our lives.
zh: 好的，我会用一种……让我们把它从育儿和工作中拿出来，因为我认为它真正展示了连接在我们所有生活中的重要性。

speaker: Unknown
en: So all of us say about our kids, "My kid doesn't listen.
zh: 所以我们所有人都会说关于我们的孩子，“我的孩子不听话。

speaker: Unknown
en: My kid doesn't listen to me."
zh: 我的孩子不听我的话。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Right?
zh: 对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: But if you picture me on the couch and let's say my... I have three kids, they're all sleeping now and I'm finally settling in for now the two minutes I have before I pass out because I'm tired.
zh: 但是如果你想象我在沙发上，假设我的……我有三个孩子，他们都睡了，我终于安顿下来度过我现在在昏睡过去之前的两分钟，因为我累了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And let's say my husband comes out and just says, "Becky, we have to do our taxes."
zh: 假设我的丈夫出来只是说，“Becky，我们必须报税。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And you're watching the scene and you see me say, "Whoa, I just sat down to read a book."
zh: 你在看着这一幕，看到我说，“哇，我刚坐下来看书。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And he goes, "You don't listen to me.
zh: 然后他说，“你不听我的话。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have a listening problem and if you don't do the taxes with me right now, I'm taking away your dessert for a week."
zh: 你有一个不听话的问题，如果你现在不跟我一起做报税，我就没收你一周的甜点。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay?
zh: 好吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Lenny, I'm pretty sure no one would tell me I have a listening problem.
zh: Lenny，我很确定没有人会说我有一个不听话的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: They probably say, I have a husband problem if that happened.
zh: 他们可能会说，我有一个丈夫的问题，如果那发生了的话。

speaker: Unknown
en: Right?
zh: 对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: They'd be like, "Wow.
zh: 他们会说，「哇。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're..."
zh: 你是……」

speaker: Unknown
en: But we do that to our kids and we do that at work all the time.
zh: 但我们总是这样对待孩子，在工作中也总是这样。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, let's say the same situation happened and he said, "Whoa, I realize didn't tell you this.
zh: 现在，假设同样的情况发生了，他说，「哇，我意识到没告诉你这件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have to get our taxes done tonight.
zh: 我们今晚必须把税务做完。

speaker: Unknown
en: You look like you're just settling in to that book.
zh: 看起来你刚安顿下来看那本书。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, can we get on top of this together?
zh: 噢，我们能一起来处理这件事吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I know we're on the same team.
zh: 我知道我们在同一战队。

speaker: Unknown
en: Can we figure this out?"
zh: 我们能解决这个问题吗？」

speaker: Unknown
en: The chances that I will do taxes just skyrocketed.
zh: 那么我去报税的可能性就会飙升。

speaker: Unknown
en: Right?
zh: 对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Why?
zh: 为什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Because he sees me in my reality as a full human being, not just as an object in his world to get something done.
zh: 因为他在我的现实中看到了我作为一个完整的人，而不仅仅是他世界里用来完成某件事的一个物体。

speaker: Unknown
en: He kind of joins my world where I'm embedded in my own priorities and by doing that forms a bridge, that's what connection is.
zh: 他某种程度上加入了我的世界，我沉浸在我自己的优先事项中，通过这样做形成了一座桥梁，这就是连接。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I can kind of walk back over to his world with him to do something that's a priority in his world.
zh: 所以我可以某种程度地走回他的世界，和他一起做某件在他世界里优先的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: So connection, it first all just feels good anyway, but it's not such a kind of soft topic.
zh: 所以连接，首先它反正就是感觉良好，但它不是一个如此软性的话题。

speaker: Unknown
en: Connection is what forms a bridge between two people so they can act together in the same interest.
zh: 连接是在两个人之间形成桥梁的东西，这样他们可以在共同利益下一起行动。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so whether you're thinking about your kid not listening or thinking about things at work, coming back to connection is really the foundation for both cooperation and at work, even productivity.
zh: 所以无论你是考虑你的孩子不听话，还是考虑工作中的事情，回到连接实际上是合作以及在工作中的效率的基础。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love the way you phrased it at the beginning, that the way you just said it, I'm like, "Oh wow, I get why someone would start."
zh: 我喜欢你在开头表述它的方式，就是你刚才说的那种方式，我就想，「噢哇，我明白为什么有人会开始了。」

speaker: Unknown
en: To listen to help people actually do this in day-to-day, what's the way to approach the way you phrase beginning that connection conversation?
zh: 为了帮助人们在日常生活中实际做到这一点而倾听，你建议的开启那种连接对话的方法是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: The truth is the first step is a mindset.
zh: 真相是，第一步是一种心态。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I know that sounds annoying because it's like, "Just tell me what to do."
zh: 我知道这听起来很烦人，因为这就像是，「直接告诉我做什么。」

speaker: Unknown
en: And anyone who knows me knows, I love telling people script ideas, but we feel people's intention, not just their intervention.
zh: 任何认识我的人都知道，我喜欢告诉人们脚本想法，但我们感觉到的是人们的意图，而不仅仅是他们的干预。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the same intervention will be felt completely different based on our mindset.
zh: 所以同样的干预会根据我们的心态感觉完全不同。

speaker: Unknown
en: So if I'm going into a conversation with you, Lenny, and let's say, I don't know, I want you to do something for me.
zh: 所以如果我和你，Lenny，进行对话，假设，我不知道，我想让你为我做些事。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'd like you to come water my non-existent plants.
zh: 我想你来给我那些不存在的植物浇水。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay?
zh: 好吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'm like, I'm going to connect to Lenny first.
zh: 而我就像，我要先与 Lenny 建立连接。

speaker: Unknown
en: First I'm going to say, "Oh, how's it going?
zh: 首先我会说，「噢，最近怎么样？

speaker: Unknown
en: Tell me this thing about your life."
zh: 跟我说说这件你生活中的事。」

speaker: Unknown
en: And you just know that is in and of itself a transaction to get you to do the thing I want you to do.
zh: 而你只知道这本身就是一种交易，为了让你做我想让你做的那件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: Not only is it not going to quote "Work," it's going to feel like dirty to you.
zh: 这不仅不会起「作用」，而且对你来说会感觉很脏。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're going to feel it and it's going to feel off.
zh: 你会感觉到它，而且它会感觉不对劲。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the mindset we need to be in, which is so hard, and I talk a good game, but it's hard for me too.
zh: 所以我们需要处于的心态是，这很难，我虽然说起来头头是道，但这对我来说也很难。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay?
zh: 好吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is trying to get into a kind of without an agenda mindset, even if it's for 30 seconds with someone.
zh: 是试图进入一种没有议程的心态，即使是与某人相处 30 秒。

speaker: Unknown
en: Being present with someone without an agenda is increasingly hard to do, but that is what connection is about.
zh: 与某人在一起而没有议程，这越来越难做到，但这就是连接的意义所在。

speaker: Unknown
en: No one wants something of me.
zh: 没有人想从我这里得到什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: They just kind of see me or they're recognizing something I'm doing or they kind of plop down on the couch next to me.
zh: 他们只是某种程度看到我，或者他们正在认可我在做的事情，或者他们某种程度上扑通一声坐在我旁边的沙发上。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so what could that look like with your kid?
zh: 那么这对你的孩子来说可能是什么样子的？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's often very, very simple things.
zh: 通常是是非常非常简单的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay?
zh: 好吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's going to feel really small, especially anyone who works a lot where we get so addicted to accomplishing and dopamine.
zh: 它会感觉真的很小，特别是对于那些工作很多的人，我们非常沉迷于成就感和多巴胺。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's sitting next to your kid and putting your hand on their back, literally, for longer than feels natural.
zh: 就是坐在你的孩子身边，把你的手放在他们的背上，字面意思，比感觉自然的时间更长。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's saying to your kid, "I'm happy to see you."
zh: 就是告诉你的孩子，「很高兴见到你。」

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's it.
zh: 就这样。

speaker: Unknown
en: Such a nice thing to say when your kid comes home from school or you come home for work or first thing in the morning.
zh: 当你的孩子放学回家，或者你下班回家，或者早上第一件事时，这是一件多么美好的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's watching your kid play and describing what they're doing instead of peppering them with questions, "Oh, you're making a tall tower."
zh: 就是看着你的孩子玩耍，描述他们在做什么，而不是用问题向他们狂轰滥炸，「噢，你在建一座高塔。」

speaker: Unknown
en: Versus, "Oh my goodness, is that a fire station?
zh: 相比于，「噢天哪，那是消防站吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh my goodness, we can make the best fire station in the world."
zh: 天哪，我们可以建造世界上最好的消防站。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I hear myself say that because I can say that, but it's not connection actually.
zh: 我听到自己这么说，因为我可以这么说，但这实际上并不是连接。

speaker: Unknown
en: At work, it's probably saying, "Hey, tell me a little bit about your long weekend."
zh: 在工作中，这可能意味着说：“嘿，跟我讲讲你的长周末。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And not kind of counting down the seconds for them to finish so you can tell them what to start on this week, but kind of giving yourself permission to say, "Can I actually just kind of drop down into that moment?"
zh: 而不是那种数着秒数等他们说完，好告诉他们这周该开始做什么，而是给自己一种许可说：“我真的可以只是沉浸到那一刻吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Right?
zh: 对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think it's the mindset and then it's actually something that doesn't have an agenda, that's just about seeing someone, noticing someone in a really, really small way.
zh: 所以我认为这是心态，然后它实际上是一种没有议程的事情，只是关于以非常、非常微小的方式看到某人、注意到某人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love this idea of just the mindset.
zh: 我喜欢这种关于心态的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's such a powerful reframe of this is don't think about what are the correct words to say.
zh: 这是一个非常有力的重构，这就是不要去想什么是正确的说法。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just actually just feel, "Okay, I just want to connect with you first for 30 seconds."
zh: 它实际上只是感觉，“好的，我只想先和你连接 30 秒钟。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I want to reveal something about myself.
zh: 我想透露一些关于我自己的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Right?
zh: 对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: So one of the things I think about is that efficiency and relationship building are often in opposition.
zh: 所以我认为的一件事是，效率和建立关系往往是相互对立的。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're doing one or the other.
zh: 我们在做其中之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: And one of the things I've learned the very, very hard way about myself, especially since I've been working, the way I've been working the last couple of years, is my efficiency is kind of reinforced at work.
zh: 我对自己学到的非常、非常痛苦的一件事，特别是自从我开始工作，以及我过去几年的工作方式，是我的效率在工作中得到了某种强化。

speaker: Unknown
en: Right?
zh: 对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: And a lot of us who can be very efficient and get things done, we can have a little morality about it.
zh: 我们许多非常高效、能完成任务的人，我们对此可能有一点道德感。

speaker: Unknown
en: We listen to someone's story, we're like, "Come on, get to the point."
zh: 我们听某人的故事，我们会说：“快点，说重点。”

speaker: Unknown
en: When that person in our life is like, "The whole point is that I want to tell you the whole story."
zh: 而当我们生活中的那个人在说：“重点就是我想把整个故事告诉你。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think if anyone's listening and thinking, "Yeah, I'm kind of an efficiency oriented person, dropping into relationship mode takes real intention."
zh: 我想如果任何人在听并且想，“是的，我是一个有点效率导向的人，进入关系模式需要真正的意图。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's hard, especially if a big part of your life, the efficiency is reinforced.
zh: 这很难，特别是如果你生活中的很大一部分，效率是被强化的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So if it feels awkward and slow.
zh: 所以如果它感觉尴尬和缓慢。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I always call it with my kids, my best time just feels low stim.
zh: 我总是和我的孩子说，我最好的时光感觉就是低刺激。

speaker: Unknown
en: It just feels so low stim.
zh: 它感觉就是很低刺激。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "Is this right?
zh: 我说：“这对吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Am I doing this right?"
zh: 我这样做对吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: That's probably a sign I am because not much is happening and I'm not being efficient with my time.
zh: 这可能是我做对的标志，因为没发生什么事，而且我在时间上并不高效。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm just in kind of relationship building mode.
zh: 我只是处于某种建立关系的模式。

speaker: Unknown
en: What I think about as you're talking about this is the best people I've worked with that are most effective at work are the people that are really good at this, that you feel like really listen, really care, just aren't rushed.
zh: 当你谈论这个时我想的是，我与之共事过的最优秀的人，那些在工作中最有效的人，就是非常擅长这个的人，你会觉得他们真的在听，真的在乎，只是不匆忙。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I could totally see the power here.
zh: 所以我完全能看到这里的力量。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So one of the core principles you teach, the name of your book and the name of your product and community and everything you built is "Good Inside."
zh: 所以你教授的核心原则之一，你的书名、你的产品名和你建立的一切社区的名字都是“Good Inside”。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm curious if that's a useful lens in the work environment too.
zh: 我好奇这在工作环境中是否也是一个有用的视角。

speaker: Unknown
en: You teach kids are good inside, no matter their behavior, they may be having a hard time, but they're still good inside.
zh: 你教导孩子内心是好的，无论他们的行为如何，他们可能正在经历困难时期，但他们内心仍然是好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is that useful in work at all?
zh: 这在工作中也有用吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Are people always still good inside?
zh: 人总是内心仍然是好的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I imagine not always.
zh: 我想象并不总是。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it's a very useful framework.
zh: 我认为这是一个非常有用的框架。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'll say why, but I want to separate things that we often confuse.
zh: 我会说原因，但我想区分我们经常混淆的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: No part of me if my kid is, when they were younger hitting, would I be like, "My kid is good inside, so who cares that they hit?"
zh: 如果我的孩子，在他们小时候打人，我绝不会说：“我的孩子内心是好的，所以谁在乎他们打人？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Or, "This employee's good inside, so it doesn't matter that they're not getting their PRDs in on time," whatever it is.
zh: 或者，“这个员工内心是好的，所以没关系他们没有按时提交 PRD，”无论是什么事。

speaker: Unknown
en: No.
zh: 不。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that's actually the other thing that's core to Good Inside is the idea of being good inside inherently requires us to separate behavior and identity.
zh: 我认为这实际上是“Good Inside”的另一个核心，即内心好的概念本质上要求我们将行为和身份区分开来。

speaker: Unknown
en: Most difficult times we have personally or interpersonally come because we've collapsed behavior and identity.
zh: 我们个人或人际之间最困难的时刻，大部分是因为我们把行为和身份混为一谈。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's easy to do because we infer a lot from people's behavior for our own.
zh: 这很容易做，因为我们从人们的行为推断很多，包括对我们自己。

speaker: Unknown
en: Right?
zh: 对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: So someone's late to work a lot, "Oh, that person's lazy."
zh: 所以某人上班经常迟到，“哦，那个人很懒。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Right?
zh: 对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Seeing that person as good inside starts with this sentence, "This is a good person who is late."
zh: 把那个人看作内心是好的，始于这句话，“这是一个迟到的好人。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Right?
zh: 对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Or, "I have a good kid who's hitting."
zh: 或者，“我有一个好孩子，但他正在打人。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I do this thing with my hands because it actually helps my brain and body see the difference.
zh: 我会用手做这个动作，因为这实际上有助于我的大脑和身体看到其中的区别。

speaker: Unknown
en: One hand is identity, the other hand is behavior and literally just separating them forces you to distinguish who someone is good inside from their behavior.
zh: 一只手代表身份，另一只手代表行为，仅仅将它们分开就迫使你去区分一个人的内在善良和他们的行为。

speaker: Unknown
en: And ironically, that's what allows you to effectively change and improve their behavior.
zh: 具有讽刺意味的是，这正是让你能够有效地改变和改善他们行为的原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because we all know what it's like to feel defensive.
zh: 因为我们都知道那种感觉防御是什么感觉。

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you know why we're defensive?
zh: 你知道我们为什么会感到防御吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Because we think someone else, instead of talking about our behavior, is talking about our identity.
zh: 因为我们认为别人在谈论我们的身份，而不是在谈论我们的行为。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then we can't even talk about our behavior anymore and being late because we feel like they're accusing us of being a bad person or being lazy or not caring about the other people in the meeting.
zh: 然后我们就甚至无法再谈论我们的行为了，比如迟到是因为我们觉得他们在指责我们是个坏人或者是懒惰，或者是根本不在乎会议里的其他人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now we can't even talk about the reason for my lateness because my whole identity feels wrapped up in it.
zh: 现在我们甚至无法谈论我迟到的原因，因为我的整个身份感觉都被卷入其中了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the quickest way to have an unproductive conversation is to lose sight of the fact that someone's good inside.
zh: 所以进行一次无成效对话的最快方式，就是忽略某人内心是善良的这一事实。

speaker: Unknown
en: If I'm talking about someone at work, let's say again, who's always late, here's like a quote "good inside," infused conversation.
zh: 如果我在谈论工作中的某个人，比方说，总是迟到的那个人，这就像是一个引用了“内心是好的”的对话。

speaker: Unknown
en: I would start by saying, "First of all, I want to say we're on the same team."
zh: 我会先说，“首先，我想说我们在同一个团队。”

speaker: Unknown
en: If that's the only thing people take from this, that is the most amazing way to have a more productive conversation with anyone.
zh: 如果这是人们从中学到的唯一一点，那这就是与任何人进行更有成效对话的最棒方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: "I want to say we're on the same team.
zh: “我想说我们在同一个团队。

speaker: Unknown
en: I know you're a good person.
zh: 我知道你是个好人。

speaker: Unknown
en: You probably don't need me to tell you that we need to start meetings on time.
zh: 你可能不需要我告诉你我们需要准时开始会议。

speaker: Unknown
en: We both know that.
zh: 我们都知道这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's also been happening consistently, which lets me know something is going on that I want to get to the bottom of with you.
zh: 这种情况也一直在发生，这让我知道有些事情正在发生，我想和你一起找到根本原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because we need you to be there at 9:00 AM to start this important leadership meeting.
zh: 因为我们需要你在上午 9:00 到场来开始这个重要的领导层会议。

speaker: Unknown
en: So tell me what's been going on.
zh: 所以告诉我发生了什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is there something at work that feels bad that makes you not want to come in?
zh: 是不是工作中有什么感觉糟糕的事情让你不想进来？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is there something going on at home?
zh: 是家里发生了什么事吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is this, I don't know, a hard time managing your time, whatever it is, let's get to the bottom of it together so we can figure it out."
zh: 是不是因为，我不知道，管理时间很困难，不管是什么，让我们一起找到根本原因，这样我们就能解决它。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So that to me is that lens.
zh: 所以对我来说，这就是那个视角。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's honestly, Lenny, it's the same thing we do, I don't know, and like a good sports coach.
zh: 而且老实说，Lenny，这和我们做的也是一样的事，我不知道，就像一个好的体育教练。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know someone, I don't know, who's a good even professional sports coach who's like, "You missed all the layups today.
zh: 我不知道某个人，我不知道，有谁是一个好的甚至职业体育教练会说，“你今天投丢了所有的上篮。

speaker: Unknown
en: What's wrong with you?
zh: 你有什么问题？

speaker: Unknown
en: And here's what I'm taking away."
zh: 这就是我要收回的东西。”

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like, "Whoa, you're a good player.
zh: 相反应该是，“哇，你是个好球员。

speaker: Unknown
en: Something's going on.
zh: 有些事情发生了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's get in the gym tomorrow.
zh: 明天我们去体育馆练练。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's figure it out.
zh: 我们来解决它。

speaker: Unknown
en: I believe in you.
zh: 我相信你。

speaker: Unknown
en: I am putting you on the bench now because it's not really working out, but we're going to get to the bottom of this together."
zh: 我现在要把你换下场，因为真的不太行，但我们要一起找到根本原因。”

speaker: Unknown
en: That comes from seeing the good inside someone.
zh: 这源于看到某人内心善良的一面。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just hearing you say these things is always like, I could see exactly why this works.
zh: 光是听你说这些，我就能清楚地明白为什么这行得通。

speaker: Unknown
en: So a takeaway here is just kind of start with admitting to them and showing them you know they are smart, they know what is the right thing to do, they just maybe aren't doing it.
zh: 所以这里的一个要点就是，首先向他们承认并向他们表明，你知道他们是聪明的，他们知道什么是正确的事情，他们只是可能没有在做而已。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And because now we can take that off the table.
zh: 而且因为现在我们可以把这一点搁置一边。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if someone's thinking, "But I don't think that of the person," then it's actually just a very different conversation, but we put that in a different category.
zh: 如果有人在想，“但我不认为那个人是那样的人”，那实际上这只是一个非常不同的对话，但我们把它放在不同的类别中。

speaker: Unknown
en: But if you start talking about someone's behavior right away and they think, which we all tend to think, "You're actually saying I'm not a smart person."
zh: 但如果你立即开始谈论某人的行为，而他们认为，我们通常都倾向于这么认为，“你实际上是在说我不够聪明。”

speaker: Unknown
en: You're not even talking about the behavior anymore.
zh: 你甚至不再谈论行为了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now their language seems like it's talking about the behavior, but all you're doing is you're having a debate you don't even realize you're having, which is whether they're a good, moral, worthy person.
zh: 现在他们的语言似乎是在谈论行为，但你实际上在做的是你在进行一场你甚至没有意识到的辩论，那就是他们是否是一个好的、有道德的、有价值的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's not an effective work conversation.
zh: 这不是一个有效的工作对话。

speaker: Unknown
en: So just kind of laying out that you know they are good, they're smart, they know the right thing to do, but here's something that isn't going the way that it should?
zh: 所以只要阐明你知道他们是好的，他们是聪明的，他们知道该做什么，但这里有些事情没有按应有的方式进行？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: This connects to something else that you teach, which is the MGI, the most generous interpretation.
zh: 这与你教授的另一件事有关，那就是 MGI，即最大善意的解读。

speaker: Unknown
en: Talk about that and how that might relate to work.
zh: 谈谈那个，以及它可能与工作有什么关系。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've realized I have an allergy to ideas that don't have action.
zh: 我意识到我对没有行动的想法过敏。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've realized this actually at work with and just with psychology too.
zh: 我在工作中也意识到了这一点，而且仅仅通过心理学也是如此。

speaker: Unknown
en: So this whole idea, people are good inside, how can you separate behavior from identity as a framework to then go into a conversation, all of it just feels too theoretical for me.
zh: 所以这整个理念，人是内心向善的，你如何将行为与身份区分为一个框架，然后再进入对话，这一切对我来说都感觉太理论化了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I like to think like, what is one tool I can use to action on that idea?
zh: 所以我喜欢思考，我能使用什么工具来对该想法采取行动？

speaker: Unknown
en: And it started, I think through parenting.
zh: 我想它是从育儿开始的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'd finished day with my kids.
zh: 我和我孩子们的一天结束了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was going to bed when my kids are [inaudible 00:22:48].
zh: 当我的孩子们[听不清 00:22:48]时，我要去睡觉了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was like, "Wow, that day was just disaster."
zh: 我心想，“哇，那天简直是一场灾难。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And then I would hear myself start talking about my kid in a way where I always loved my kid, but I didn't realize till later, even through the language, I stopped liking my kid.
zh: 然后我会听到自己开始谈论我的孩子，以一种我一直爱我的孩子的方式，但我直到后来才意识到，即使通过语言，我不再喜欢我的孩子了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was just.
zh: 我只是。

speaker: Unknown
en: Even through the language, I stopped liking my kid.
zh: 即使通过语言，我不再喜欢我的孩子了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was just listening to your Peter Dang episode, which was a lot about language and how language then impacts thoughts.
zh: 我刚才听了你的 Peter Dang 那一期节目，那期很多是关于语言以及语言如何影响思想的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think, a lot as a parent, but it's true as a leader, the story you tell yourself as a kid at night, kind of becomes the parent you are the next morning.
zh: 我认为，作为父母很多时候是这样，但对于领导者来说也是如此，你在晚上对自己讲述关于那个孩子的故事，某种程度上成为了你第二天早上的父母模样。

speaker: Unknown
en: Probably same truth.
zh: 可能是同样的道理。

speaker: Unknown
en: The story you tell yourself by your organization at night, becomes the leader you are the next morning.
zh: 你在晚上对自己讲述关于你组织的故事，成为了你第二天早上的领导者模样。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I realized I was using what I later called this least generous interpretation.
zh: 我意识到我正在使用我后来称之为这种“最不宽容解读”的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think we all do that naturally, at least I do.
zh: 我想我们都会自然而然地那样做，至少我是这样。

speaker: Unknown
en: Like, I see my kid not listening and laughing if I'm trying to discipline.
zh: 比如，我看到我的孩子不听话而且在我试图管教时大笑。

speaker: Unknown
en: And my first thought is, I think my kid's a sociopath.
zh: 我的第一个念头是，我觉得我的孩子是个反社会的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know how I get there.
zh: 我不知道我是怎么得出那个结论的。

speaker: Unknown
en: My kid's two years old, but it happens so fast.
zh: 我的孩子才两岁，但这发生得太快了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay?
zh: 好吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And I realized at night, the only way to shift this and get into a good inside mindset, was just to say to myself, what's the most generous interpretation of why I would say to my kids some version of, stop jumping on the couch, it's dangerous, you're near the glass table, and he'd look at me and jump doubly hard on the couch.
zh: 我意识到在晚上，转变这种状态并进入内心向善的思维模式的唯一方法，就是对自己说，我对我会对我孩子们说的某些话做最宽容的解读是什么，比如不要在沙发上跳，这很危险，你靠近玻璃桌了，而他看着我，并在沙发上跳得加倍用力。

speaker: Unknown
en: My least generous interpretation was that he's sociopath.
zh: 我的最不宽容解读是他是个反社会的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I already covered that one.
zh: 我已经涵盖了那一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: My most generous interpretation, there's not one right answer.
zh: 我的最宽容解读，没有一个正确答案。

speaker: Unknown
en: It might be, this is a kid who's really oriented around wanting to feel in control.
zh: 可能是，这是一个非常围绕着想要感到掌控感的孩子。

speaker: Unknown
en: And when that feels threatened, he doubles down.
zh: 而当那感到受到威胁时，他就会加倍坚持。

speaker: Unknown
en: He is 0% people pleasing, this third child of mine, zero.
zh: 他是 0% 取悦他人的，我的这第三个孩子，零。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the whole, I'm disappointed with you, it's not going to work with a kid like that.
zh: 所以整个，“我对你很失望”这一套，对这样的孩子是不会起作用的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And when I started using an MGI, a couple things happened.
zh: 当我开始使用一个 MGI（最宽容解读）时，发生了几件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: I realized I liked my kid again.
zh: 我意识到我又喜欢我的孩子了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think we don't talk about that enough as parents, that that's the thing that keeps us up at night.
zh: 我认为作为父母我们对此讨论得不够，这才是让我们夜不能寐的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not their behavior.
zh: 不是他们的行为。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's that we slowly stop liking them based on how we're describing them.
zh: 而是我们基于我们描述他们的方式，慢慢地不再喜欢他们了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then I realized we were on the same team.
zh: 然后我意识到我们在同一条战线上。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then I came up with a whole different range of interventions because I used a most generous interpretation.
zh: 然后我想出了完全不同的一系列干预措施，因为我使用了最宽容解读。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think the same thing is true at work.
zh: 我认为同样的事情在工作中也是真实的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Someone, I don't know, is belaboring their point in a meeting, when everyone else has gone on.
zh: 某人，我不知道，在会议上纠缠于他的观点，而其他人都已经继续进行了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And maybe the least generous interpretation, maybe is what I was reacting to in your initial question is, oh, they're being a baby or everyone just is vain.
zh: 也许最不宽容解读，也许是你最初问题中我正在反应的，是，哦，他们在表现得像个宝宝，或者每个人都只是虚荣。

speaker: Unknown
en: My most generous interpretation, I don't know, could be a variety of things.
zh: 我的最宽容解读，我不知道，可能是各种各样的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Did they not feel heard the first time?
zh: 他们第一次没有感觉到被倾听吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Still doesn't make them going on forever, acceptable.
zh: 这仍然不能让他们永远讲下去变得可以被接受。

speaker: Unknown
en: But if that's true, I might say to them in private, Hey, something happens in meetings where I wonder if you don't always feel heard.
zh: 但如果那是真的，我可能会私下对他们说，嘿，在会议中发生了一些事情，我想知道你是否总是感觉没有被倾听。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then you keep talking and then honestly, the rest of the room gets annoyed, which makes us tune you out more, which probably makes you belabor your point more.
zh: 然后你继续讲，然后老实说，房间里的其他人都会感到恼火，这让我们更不理会你，这可能反而让你更纠缠于你的观点。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're in a bad cycle.
zh: 我们陷入了一个糟糕的循环。

speaker: Unknown
en: Can we work together on changing this?
zh: 我们可以一起努力改变这个吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And now all of a sudden, instead of everyone complaining about that person or whatever it is, and nothing changing, which is just horrible for culture and productivity, we have one conversation through an MGI lens and things start moving forward in a more productive way.
zh: 现在突然间，不再是每个人都抱怨那个人或不管是什么，而且没有任何改变，这对文化和生产力来说简直是可怕的，我们通过 MGI 镜头进行了一次对话，事情开始以更富有成效的方式向前发展。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's interesting how much of this comes back to just kind of assuming they're smart and are trying to do their best job and are good inside and translating to, okay, in spite of that, here's something that is still going wrong, let's try to figure out... There's kind of this idea of curiosity over judgment, trying to figure out what's missing.
zh: 有趣的是有多少事情回到仅仅是假设他们是聪明的，并试图尽力做好工作，并且内心是向善的，并转化为，好的，尽管如此，这里有些事情仍然出了问题，让我们试着弄清楚……有一种好奇胜过评判的想法，试图弄清楚缺少了什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I mean, that's exactly it.
zh: 我的意思是，确实就是这样。

speaker: Unknown
en: Those are two things also that I think are in opposition.
zh: 这也是我认为对立的两件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: You inherently cannot be judgmental when you're curious.
zh: 当你感到好奇时，你天生就无法做出评判。

speaker: Unknown
en: And when you're judgmental about something, you're inherently not curious about it.
zh: 当你对某事做出评判时，你天生就对它没有好奇心。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think it's assuming... I say assume, good inside or assume MGI, other people say assume positive intent.
zh: 我认为这假设……我说假设，内心良善或假设 MGI，其他人说假设积极意图。

speaker: Unknown
en: Whatever language hits your heart, I think is the language that's... Everyone's different.
zh: 无论哪种语言能触动你的心，我认为就是那种……每个人都是不同的。

speaker: Unknown
en: MGI makes sense for me, but if there's someone else that has a different phrase, someone should use that one.
zh: MGI 对我来说有意义，但如果其他人有不同的短语，他们应该使用那一个。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then, I think also assuming, this person would want to work through this with me.
zh: 然后，我认为也要假设，这个人想和我一起解决这个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think Lenny, that's one of the things that we also miss a ton in our kids.
zh: 我认为 Lenny，这也是我们在孩子身上经常遗漏的一件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: The kids who hit, no matter what they say, they really don't like feeling out of control.
zh: 那些打人的孩子，不管他们说什么，他们真的不喜欢失控的感觉。

speaker: Unknown
en: The kid who's lying to your face, they don't want to lie to your face.
zh: 当面撒谎的孩子，不想对你当面撒谎。

speaker: Unknown
en: They don't want to behave this way either, again, which doesn't make the behavior okay, but it makes us soften a tiny bit and realize we're on the same team.
zh: 他们也不想这样表现，再次强调，这并不能让这种行为变得可以接受，但这让我们稍微软化一点，并意识到我们在同一个团队。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think actually we all want the same outcome.
zh: 我认为实际上我们都想要同样的结果。

speaker: Unknown
en: Wait, say more about that, because that doesn't feel, as a parent, that my kid doesn't want to... When he's throwing a thing across the way or just resisting going in the car.
zh: 等等，多说说这一点，因为作为父母，感觉我的孩子不想……当他把东西扔过房间或者拒绝上车时。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to hear more about what's going on there.
zh: 我想听听更多关于那里发生了什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, first of all, I'll start with the baseline, right?
zh: 我的意思是，首先，我将从基线开始，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: That as a clinical psychologist, I was initially trained in this very reward, punishment, timeout, sticker chart, mode.
zh: 作为一名临床心理学家，我最初接受的是这种非常基于奖赏、惩罚、暂停、贴纸图表模式的训练。

speaker: Unknown
en: And to be honest, I loved it, because I have a very healthy left brain and our left brains love logic and we love linearity.
zh: 老实说，我很喜欢它，因为我有一个非常健康的左脑，而我们的左脑热爱逻辑，我们热爱线性。

speaker: Unknown
en: And as soon as you're a parent, you're like, there's no linearity or logic really, but our brain wants that to be a system.
zh: 一旦你成为父母，你就会觉得，真的没有线性或逻辑，但我们的大脑希望它成为一个系统。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's kind of a system of punish the bad and you have less of the bad and reward the good.
zh: 这是一种惩罚坏事，坏事就会减少，奖励好事的系统。

speaker: Unknown
en: And no one really says, Well, how are we raising a human?
zh: 没有人真的说，嗯，我们要如何培养一个人？

speaker: Unknown
en: Those are just behaviors, but our brain loves it.
zh: 那些只是行为，但我们的大脑喜欢它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's what I started teaching to parents, I did, in my private practice, because it felt clean.
zh: 这就是我开始教给父母的，我在私人执业中做的，因为它感觉很干净。

speaker: Unknown
en: But it was at complete odds with everything I knew was helping all the adults in my practice change their lives.
zh: 但这与我所知道的帮助我执业中所有成年人改变生活的一切都完全不一致。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's actually what gave me insight into starting Good Inside.
zh: 这实际上就是我创办 Good Inside 的洞见来源。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like, there's no way that what helps the 35-year-old rewire their brain and change for the better, is that theoretical opposition with what a two-year-old and five-year-old needs to become resilient and confident.
zh: 就像，帮助 35 岁的人重组大脑并变得更好的东西，与 2 岁和 5 岁的孩子变得有韧性和自信所需的东西在理论上是对立的，这是不可能的。

speaker: Unknown
en: That just doesn't make sense.
zh: 这根本说不通。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think we have these ideas about kids.
zh: 我认为我们对孩子有这些看法。

speaker: Unknown
en: Even something I hear a lot, you're making a bad choice, to kids.
zh: 甚至是我经常听到的一句话，你在做一个糟糕的选择，对孩子说。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're a good person, you're making a bad choice.
zh: 你是一个好人，你在做一个糟糕的选择。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is not about forgiving bad behavior, I'm pretty firm about that.
zh: 这不是关于原谅不良行为，我对此非常坚定。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I just don't believe a four-year-old who's throwing is like, hold on, should I throw this at my sister?
zh: 但我就是不相信一个正在扔东西的 4 岁孩子会想，等等，我应该把这个扔向我姐姐吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Should I or should I not?
zh: 我应该还是不应该？

speaker: Unknown
en: I will choose as not... I mean, think about us.
zh: 我会选择不……我的意思是，想想我们自己。

speaker: Unknown
en: When we act out, I don't think most of us are in a decision making place.
zh: 当我们发泄时，我认为我们大多数人并不处于做决定的状态。

speaker: Unknown
en: We escalate and here's one of the big insights besides people are good inside, that helped me form all of my other approaches, is kids are born with all the feelings and none of the skills to manage feelings.
zh: 我们升级了，除了人内心良善之外，这里有一个帮助我形成所有其他方法的重要洞见，孩子生来就带着所有的感觉，却没有管理感觉的技能。

speaker: Unknown
en: Bad behavior at any age can basically be reduced to feelings that overpower skills.
zh: 任何年龄的不良行为基本上都可以归结为感觉压倒了技能。

speaker: Unknown
en: And yes, behavior is a problem, but behavior isn't the core problem.
zh: 是的，行为是个问题，但行为不是核心问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a manifestation of the problem.
zh: 它是问题的表现形式。

speaker: Unknown
en: The actual problem is someone doesn't have the skill they need to manage something happening internally.
zh: 真正的问题是某人没有管理内部发生的事情所需的技能。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if they had that skill and could access it, the behavior would change.
zh: 如果他们有那个技能并且能够使用它，行为就会改变。

speaker: Unknown
en: But that would be kind of the outcome, not the place of actual intervention.
zh: 但那将是结果，而不是实际干预的位置。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, when a kid hits, they look also and kids will say things like, "I don't care what you say."
zh: 所以，当一个孩子打人时，他们看起来也像，孩子们会说像这样的话，「我不在乎你说什么。」

speaker: Unknown
en: They're out of control.
zh: 他们失控了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe they're in a situation where, I don't know, they could be tired, but also they're angry that a sibling has a toy and they don't have skills to manage anger, they're jealous that someone is playing with something and they can't manage the jealousy.
zh: 也许他们处于一种情况，我不知道，他们可能累了，但也因为兄弟姐妹有一个玩具而生气，他们没有管理愤怒的技能，他们嫉妒有人在玩某样东西，他们无法管理嫉妒。

speaker: Unknown
en: And when you look at bad behavior through the lens of feelings that overpower skills, you start to think like a coach.
zh: 当你通过感觉压倒技能的视角来看待不良行为时，你开始像一个教练那样思考。

speaker: Unknown
en: It always has struck me with kids, that we don't punish them into learning how to swim, which I know sounds funny.
zh: 这一直让我对孩子感到震惊，我们不会通过惩罚让他们学会游泳，我知道这听起来很好笑。

speaker: Unknown
en: But if you paid for swim lessons and the person said, You know what?
zh: 但是如果你付了游泳课的费用，那个人说，你知道吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm just not going to put up with this.
zh: 我就是不想忍受这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's this inappropriate behavior.
zh: 这是不当行为。

speaker: Unknown
en: By the way, your kid has to learn to swim if they're going to function in life.
zh: 顺便说一句，如果你们的孩子想在生活中运作，就必须学会游泳。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, send them to their room and tell them to come back when they learn how to swim.
zh: 所以，把他们送回房间，告诉他们学会游泳后再回来。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's absurd, it's obviously laughable.
zh: 这很荒谬，显然很可笑。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think swimming is very important, but an even more important life skill is learning how to manage your emotions.
zh: 我认为游泳很重要，但更重要的是生活技能是学习如何管理你的情绪。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is.
zh: 确实如此。

speaker: Unknown
en: And nobody learns new skills by being sent to their room, nobody learns new skills by adding shame.
zh: 没有人通过被送回房间来学习新技能，没有人通过增加羞耻感来学习新技能。

speaker: Unknown
en: All that does is increase the gap between feelings and skills.
zh: 这样做只会增加感觉和技能之间的差距。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just totally counterproductive.
zh: 这完全是适得其反的。

speaker: Unknown
en: The answer is, we have to set boundaries.
zh: 答案是，我们必须设定界限。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have to be the people setting boundaries around out of control behavior.
zh: 我们必须成为为失控行为设定界限的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: But then we actually have to teach our kids the skills they were missing, which levels up the skills.
zh: 但随后我们实际上必须教导孩子们他们所缺乏的技能，这能提升技能。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's how not only you change behavior short term, but you actually change behavior massively long term.
zh: 这就是你不仅在短期内改变行为，而且实际上在长期内大幅改变行为的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm actually in the middle of starting potty training with our kid and I'm taking this little online course and they call it potty learning, not potty training, for exactly that reason.
zh: 我实际上正在开始对我们孩子进行如厕训练，我正在参加这个小型的在线课程，他们称之为如厕学习，而不是如厕训练，正是出于那个原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're not training them to do a thing.
zh: 你不是在训练他们做某事。

speaker: Unknown
en: They don't know how to do it, so you just teach them, like you're teaching them the alphabet, just teach them all little steps and what they need to learn.
zh: 他们不知道怎么做，所以你只要教他们，就像你在教他们字母表一样，只要教他们所有的小步骤和他们需要学习的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're not doing our potty learning course?
zh: 你没有参加我们的如厕学习课程吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Lenny, I'm going to talk to you after.
zh: Lenny，我待会儿会跟你谈。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, I didn't know you had one.
zh: 哦，我不知道你有一个。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Our marketing message isn't getting out there.
zh: 我们的营销信息还没有传达出去。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm switching immediately.
zh: 我立刻要转投。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, how do people find it?
zh: 嗯，人们怎么找到它？

speaker: Unknown
en: Goodinside.com?
zh: Goodinside.com？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's actually one of the things I feel very strongly about putting out for free.
zh: 这实际上是我非常强烈认为应该免费提供的东西之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because, I think potty stuff is one of the early lessons around body autonomy and skills.
zh: 因为我认为如厕的事情是关于身体自主权和技能的早期课程之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the quickest way we kind of mess it up as parents is kind of trying to take over the process.
zh: 而我们作为父母搞砸它的最快方式是试图接管这个过程。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it could be so stressful.
zh: 这可能会非常有压力。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I actually told my team while ago is, I just think this should be a service.
zh: 我实际上不久前告诉我的团队的是，我只是认为这应该是一项服务。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, if you Google Good Inside potty, you can have it for zero dollars.
zh: 所以，如果你 Google Good Inside potty，你可以零美元获得它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, it's actually free already.
zh: 哦，它实际上已经是免费的了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes, it's free.
zh: 是的，它是免费的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm switching immediately.
zh: 我立刻要转投。

speaker: Dr. Becky Kennedy
en: Yes.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is there a corollary to adult work environments in potty learning?
zh: 在如厕学习方面，成人的工作环境是否有类似的类比？

speaker: Dr. Becky Kennedy
en: Yeah. There is, there always is.
zh: 是的。有，总有的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Do tell.
zh: 请讲。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, okay.
zh: 嗯，好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, let's think about what the potty stuff is about for kids.
zh: 那么，让我们思考一下如厕的事情对孩子来说意味着什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: When our kids are younger, they control two things in their entire life.
zh: 当我们的孩子还小的时候，他们只控制生活中的两件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: What goes into their body and what comes out of their body?
zh: 什么进入他们的身体，什么排出他们的身体？

speaker: Unknown
en: That's all.
zh: 就这些。

speaker: Unknown
en: No, obviously, hopefully we let them pick their clothes here and there, but thematically... By the way, and they shouldn't be in charge of a ton more categories because they're two, right?
zh: 不，显然，希望我们让他们偶尔挑挑衣服，但在主题上……顺便说一句，他们不应该再负责更多的类别，因为他们才两岁，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: But it's why those two areas can become so heated for families because we get so stressed.
zh: 但这就是为什么这两个领域对家庭来说可能会变得如此充满火药味，因为我们变得非常有压力。

speaker: Unknown
en: And when kids, especially if you don't have a people pleaser, okay?
zh: 而当孩子，特别是如果你没有一个人取悦者，好吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: When kids even smell, my parent is stepping into one of the only two domains that are mine, holy moly, will they do nutty things to push you back.
zh: 当孩子甚至感觉到，我的父母正在踏入仅有的两个属于我的领域之一时，天哪，他们会做疯狂的事情把你推回去。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, what's the general lesson, the human lesson is, what is it like for someone to have not that many areas of life that they're in control of?
zh: 所以，一般的教训，人类的教训是，对于一个人在生活中没有那么多控制领域是什么感觉？

speaker: Unknown
en: Number one, just think about that.
zh: 第一，就想一想那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: Think about the first 10 minutes of your kids' day.
zh: 想想你孩子一天的前 10 分钟。

speaker: Unknown
en: We wake them up.
zh: 我们叫醒他们。

speaker: Unknown
en: We tell them what the weather is.
zh: 我们告诉他们天气是什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: We tell them, No, you can't wear that.
zh: 我们告诉他们，不，你不能穿那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: We tell them what's for breakfast.
zh: 我们告诉他们早餐吃什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: We tell them their shirt's on the wrong way.
zh: 我们告诉他们他们的衬衫穿反了。

speaker: Unknown
en: If my husband woke me up that way, I would be in a bad mood the rest of the day.
zh: 如果我丈夫那样叫醒我，我这一天余下的时间都会心情不好。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay?
zh: 好吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'd probably be looking for a million different areas to just grasp control of as a way of screaming to the world like, I am my own person.
zh: 而且我可能会找一百万个不同的地方来试图抓住控制权，作为向世界呐喊的一种方式，就像说，我是我自己的主人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Right?
zh: 对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, if we think about that in the workplace, well, first of all, temperamentally, there are a subset of people, I call these the resilient rebels.
zh: 所以，如果我们在职场中思考这个问题，嗯，首先，从性情上来说，有一群人，我称这些人为坚韧的叛逆者。

speaker: Unknown
en: They present as strong-willed, defiant kids, whose... I think we all walk around with the core fear that we end up acting out.
zh: 他们表现为意志坚定、反抗的孩子，他们的……我觉得我们所有人都带着一种核心恐惧在四处走动，最终我们会表现出来。

speaker: Unknown
en: Their core fear is the loss of control.
zh: 他们的核心恐惧是失去控制。

speaker: Unknown
en: They are very, very attuned to people kind of stepping into their domain, and if they smell it, they resist.
zh: 他们非常非常敏感，能够察觉人们踏入他们的领域，如果他们闻到了这种味道，他们就会抵抗。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, we have those as adults too.
zh: 嗯，我们成年人中也有这样的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's tricky to collaborate with them.
zh: 与他们合作是很棘手的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And again, we need to have an expectation, but we might see that as, why do I ask someone to switch a meeting time?
zh: 而且，我们需要有一个预期，但我们可能会把它看作为什么我要求某人更改会议时间？

speaker: Unknown
en: And it feels like I took a knife to their heart, the way they react, right?
zh: 感觉就像我拿刀捅进了他们的心脏，看他们反应的方式，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, that theme might be coming up and maybe we can go one level deeper and say, Is this someone who in general... I'm making this up, but, oh, someone got promoted before they did on their team, they just had a change in manager, they just had their vacation denied, whatever it was.
zh: 嗯，那个主题可能会出现，也许我们可以深入一层，说，这是否是一个总体上……我是瞎编的，但是，哦，团队里有人在某人之前得到了晋升，他们刚换了经理，他们的休假被拒了，不管是什么事。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, is that actually what we're talking about?
zh: 哦，这实际上就是我们在讨论的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's manifesting as this difficulty changing meaning time, but actually there's other themes of control and independence at play.
zh: 它表现为这种更改时间的困难，但实际上还有其他的控制和独立的主题在起作用。

speaker: Unknown
en: As maybe a lesson here is, clarifying, here's what you own on this team, on this project, here's things that you're going to drive, here's where I'm going to be really involved.
zh: 也许这儿的教训是，澄清这一点，这是你在这个团队、这个项目中拥有的，这是你要驱动的事情，这是我要非常深入参与的地方。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is that maybe a takeaway?
zh: 这可能是一个收获吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that is definitely a takeaway.
zh: 我认为这绝对是一个收获。

speaker: Unknown
en: I also just love naming your intention, that's always really helpful.
zh: 我也真的很喜欢说出你的意图，这总是很有帮助。

speaker: Unknown
en: Hey, I want to look over this project together, because I want to get ahead of some things and I think that's actually going to help you do the most independent work.
zh: 嘿，我想一起看看这个项目，因为我想在事情发生前做好准备，我认为这实际上将帮助你完成最独立的工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's why I want to go through it in such detail.
zh: 这就是为什么我想如此详细地过一遍。

speaker: Unknown
en: When you name the intention very, very clearly for someone, they're much more likely to interpret your behavior through the intention you just named, because your intention isn't to control them or make them feel small.
zh: 当你为某人非常非常清楚地说明意图时，他们更有可能通过你刚才说明的意图来解读你的行为，因为你的意图不是控制他们或让他们感到渺小。

speaker: Unknown
en: But if that's also something they have a predilection for, it's even more important for those people to do the naming of the intention up front.
zh: 但如果这也是他们有偏好的一种东西，那么对于这些人来说，预先说明意图就更为重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: And there's also just a whole micromanaging, I don't know, element to this.
zh: 而且这里还有整个微管理，我不知道，这一方面。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, this is why people in part hate micromanagement.
zh: 所以，这就是人们部分讨厌微管理的原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: Like, I want to be in control of something, why get out of this?
zh: 就像，我想控制某事，为什么要摆脱这个？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, which is a dance.
zh: 是的，这是一种舞蹈。

speaker: Unknown
en: Exactly.
zh: 确切地说。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Something else that you teach, that I find really helpful, is this idea of sturdy, becoming a sturdy parent, learning to be sturdy.
zh: 你教的另一件事我觉得非常有帮助，就是这种坚定的观念，成为一个坚定的父母，学习变得坚定。

speaker: Unknown
en: That feels very related to being a good leader, just like learning how to deal with challenges.
zh: 这感觉与成为一个好的领导者非常相关，就像学习如何应对挑战一样。

speaker: Unknown
en: Talk about that from the kids' context and then how it might be useful in work.
zh: 从孩子的语境谈论这一点，然后谈谈它在工作中如何有用。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, I think that's the essence of what Good Inside does.
zh: 是的，我认为这就是 Good Inside 所做工作的精髓。

speaker: Unknown
en: We help parents become sturdy leaders, so they can raise sturdy, confident kids.
zh: 我们帮助父母成为坚定的领导者，这样他们就能抚养出坚定、自信的孩子。

speaker: Unknown
en: And when I started this, I'd use that word a lot.
zh: 当我开始这项工作时，我会经常使用这个词。

speaker: Unknown
en: People would say to me, I've never heard anyone use that word.
zh: 人们会对我说，我从未听任何人使用过那个词。

speaker: Unknown
en: I know what it means.
zh: 我知道它的意思。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I think about language too, and I like language that helps me conjure up an image or a feeling.
zh: 但我也思考语言，我喜欢那种能帮助我构思出一个形象或感觉的语言。

speaker: Unknown
en: It takes it out of my brain and I think the word sturdy, if you picture someone in your life, even if you haven't thought about them, who's the sturdy person I know?
zh: 它把它从我的大脑中带出来，我认为坚定这个词，如果你想象你生活中的一个人，即使你没有想过他们，谁是我认识的坚定的人？

speaker: Unknown
en: I bet you can kind of locate them, you see them, and there's a feeling you have around them.
zh: 我打赌你可以稍微找到他们，你看到他们，而且你对他们有一种感觉。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think the best leaders we have are sturdy.
zh: 我认为我们拥有的最好的领导者是坚定的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, I'll start it with a metaphor, because I actually think that brings it to life.
zh: 所以，我会用一个隐喻来开始，因为我实际上认为这让它生动起来。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think this is as true in parenting and in the workplace.
zh: 我认为这在育儿和工作场所中都是如此真实。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, picture being a passenger on a flight.
zh: 所以，想象自己是飞机上的乘客。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's really turbulent and you start screaming and everyone starts screaming, okay?
zh: 颠簸非常剧烈，你开始尖叫，每个人都开始尖叫，好吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: So, everyone's panicked in the flight and then you hear the announcement from the pilot and I think there's three types of announcements you can have.
zh: 所以，飞机上的每个人都惊慌失措，然后你听到了飞行员的广播，我认为有三种类型的广播。

speaker: Unknown
en: The third one's going to be the sturdy one, the first two, not as much.
zh: 第三种将是坚定的那种，前两种则不那么坚定。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the first one is some version of, What are you freaking out about?
zh: 所以第一个版本是某种形式的，你到底在慌什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Stop screaming.
zh: 别再尖叫了。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're making this flight awful for everyone and you're distracting me and stop making a big deal out of nothing.
zh: 你让这趟飞行对每个人来说都很糟糕，而且你在让我分心，别把没什么大不了的事闹得很大。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's kind of when we say those words to a kid, Stop freaking out.
zh: 这就是当我们对孩子说那些话的时候，别再慌了。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're ruining this for everyone.
zh: 你正在为大家毁了这一切。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the truth is, if you think about yourself on a flight and the pilot says that, you don't feel calmer.
zh: 但事实是，如果你想想自己在飞机上，飞行员说了那样的话，你不会感觉更平静。

speaker: Unknown
en: Number one, you're worried they don't notice the turbulence.
zh: 第一，你担心他们没有注意到湍流。

speaker: Unknown
en: Number two, you're a little disturbed that it just takes your screaming to make the pilot kind of lose it at you, right?
zh: 第二，你有点被惊扰，因为仅仅因为你的尖叫就让飞行员有点对你失控了，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: And that actually makes you feel less safe.
zh: 这实际上让你感到不那么安全。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, that's like in a meeting where people, I don't know, voice something that doesn't feel good and the leader essentially is like, Stop complaining.
zh: 所以，这就像是在一个会议里，人们，我不知道，表达了某种让人不舒服的感觉，而领导者本质上就像是在说，别再抱怨了。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's not sturdy.
zh: 那并不稳健。

speaker: Unknown
en: The second version is also not sturdy.
zh: 第二个版本也不稳健。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think we've a little bit, at least in parenting, over corrected to this, which is a pilot saying like, I hear you screaming.
zh: 我认为我们有点，至少在育儿方面，过度纠正到了这一点，也就是飞行员说，我听到你在尖叫。

speaker: Unknown
en: You know what?
zh: 你知道吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm opening the cockpit door.
zh: 我要打开驾驶舱门了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Does anyone want to fly the plane because your screaming is make me anxious and I'm not really sure what to do.
zh: 有人想开飞机吗？因为你的尖叫让我很焦虑，我也不太知道该怎么办。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe one of you want to do it.
zh: 也许你们当中有人想来做这件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that's a massive overcorrection I've seen in parenting and I just want to make it very clear that is not what we do at Good Inside, which is kind of, my kid is upset and instead of just caring about their feelings, now their feelings dictate my decisions.
zh: 我认为这是我在育儿中看到的一个巨大的过度矫正，我只想非常明确，这不是我们在 Good Inside 所做的，这有点像是，我的孩子心烦意乱，与其只是关心他们的感受，现在他们的感受支配了我的决定。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's terrifying for someone on a flight.
zh: 对于飞机上的某人来说，这太可怕了。

speaker: Unknown
en: If that's me, I'm not even scared of the turbulence anymore.
zh: 如果那是我，我甚至不再害怕湍流了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm scared that this person is my supposed leader.
zh: 我害怕这个人是我的所谓领导者。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's also in a meeting when you complain about things and a leader might say, Oh, okay, you know what?
zh: 这也在一个会议中，当你抱怨事情时，领导者可能会说，噢，好的，你知道吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, we'll just get consensus on this.
zh: 好的，我们就在这事上达成共识吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: When it's a decision, that's a leadership decision, not a consensus decision, which some decisions are, but some decisions aren't because you are the only one who has certain information.
zh: 当这是一个决定时，这是一个领导决策，而不是共识决策，有些决策是，但有些不是，因为你是唯一拥有某些信息的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, to me is the third announcement that I want to hear, when it's really turbulent, is something like this.
zh: 现在，对我来说，我想听的第三个通告是，当真的有湍流时，是这样的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I hear everyone screaming back there, that makes sense.
zh: 我听到后面大家在尖叫，这很有道理。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you haven't been in as many flights, you're right, it's very bumpy.
zh: 而且你们坐过的航班没这么多，你是对的，这非常颠簸。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I know what I'm doing.
zh: 而且我知道我在做什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: This turbulence, though it scares you, it doesn't scare me.
zh: 这种湍流，虽然它吓到了你，但它不吓到我。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm actually going to get off this loudspeaker right now to go back to do my job.
zh: 我现在要挂断这个扩音器回去做我的工作了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'll see you when we land in Los Angeles.
zh: 等我们在洛杉矶降落时再见。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, even if the turbulence is the same, all of a sudden I feel this deep breath, because my worry is contained.
zh: 现在，即使湍流还是一样，突然我感到深呼吸，因为我的担心被遏制了。

speaker: Unknown
en: This sturdy leader is able to see my emotional experience as real for me and not be overwhelmed by it themself.
zh: 这个稳健的领导者能够将我的情感体验视为对我来说真实的，而他们自己不会被它压倒。

speaker: Unknown
en: Those are the two distinct aspects of being a sturdy leader.
zh: 这些是做一个稳健的领导者的两个不同方面。

speaker: Unknown
en: I can see someone else's experience as real for them, but I can still hold onto my experience and so I'm not overwhelmed by someone else.
zh: 我可以将别人的体验视为对他们来说是真实的，但我仍然可以坚持我自己的体验，所以我不会被别人压倒。

speaker: Unknown
en: So to a kid, it might be saying, look, let's say TV time is over, and my kid does not [inaudible 00:39:42] in my house, when I say TV time is over, I'm turning off the TV.
zh: 所以对孩子来说，可能是说，看，假设看电视的时间结束了，而且我的孩子没有[听不清 00:39:42]在我家里，当我说看电视时间结束时，我会关掉电视。

speaker: Unknown
en: When my kids were younger, they didn't say, You're right, mom, that's a great decision.
zh: 当我的孩子更小的时候，他们不会说，你是对的，妈妈，这是一个很棒的决定。

speaker: Unknown
en: No.
zh: 不。

speaker: Unknown
en: They freaked out because they wanted to watch another one.
zh: 他们会崩溃，因为他们想再看一个。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, a sturdy leader would say in that position, I'm turning the TV off, that's my decision.
zh: 所以，一个稳健的领导者在那个位置会说，我要关掉电视，那是我的决定。

speaker: Unknown
en: I get that you're upset, I don't really like to end TV time either.
zh: 我明白你心烦意乱，我也不喜欢结束看电视的时间。

speaker: Unknown
en: I also know we'll get through it.
zh: 我也知道我们会度过它的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Do your thing and then we'll transition to brushing teeth and then I kind of wait it out.
zh: 做你的事，然后我们会过渡到刷牙，然后我就稍微等着它过去。

speaker: Unknown
en: Or in the workplace, someone's complaining about, I don't know, making this up, but maybe it's some policy change, vacation change, number of days in the office, where you say, look, I want to share something that I know some people are going to have a reaction to, here's the new policy.
zh: 或者在工作场所，有人在抱怨，我不知道，这是我编的，但也许是某种政策变动，休假变动，办公室的天数，你说，看，我想分享一些我知道有些人会有反应的事情，这是新政策。

speaker: Unknown
en: People stop [inaudible 00:40:25], complaining.
zh: 人们停止[听不清 00:40:25]，抱怨。

speaker: Unknown
en: Look, I get it, this is a big change and you're right.
zh: 看，我明白，这是一个很大的变化，你是对的。

speaker: Unknown
en: This does make things hard in a certain way, and I totally see that, and the way you're feeling makes sense.
zh: 这确实在某种方面让事情变得困难，我完全看到这一点，而且你的感觉是有道理的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And here's some version of why we're doing it and I know we're going to get through it and I have faith in us to weather this turbulence.
zh: 这里有某种版本的我们为什么要做这件事，我知道我们会度过它的，我有信心我们能经受住这场湍流。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think, in every situation, kids, workplace, in laws, whatever it is, that sturdy leadership always feels good.
zh: 我认为，在每种情况下，孩子，工作场所，姻亲，不管是什么，那种稳健的领导力总是让人感觉良好。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is a really good segue to something I definitely wanted to ask you about, which is this idea of boundaries, of how to set boundaries well and how that relates to long-term resilience, versus just this idea of short-term happiness.
zh: 这是一个非常好的过渡，衔接我确实想问你的事情，也就是关于界限的这个想法，如何很好地设定界限，以及这如何与长期的韧性相关联，而不是仅仅关于短期幸福的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was just yesterday watching your advice on how to set boundaries with my kid, because I struggle with it sometimes and I'm just like, What am I doing wrong?
zh: 我昨天还在看你关于如何与孩子设定界限的建议，因为我有时在这方面很挣扎，我就想，我做错了什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: And it was extremely helpful.
zh: 那非常有帮助。

speaker: Unknown
en: I started using your advice and I'm like, Wow, he's doing exactly what I wanted to do.
zh: 我开始采纳你的建议，然后我就想，哇，他完全按照我的意愿去做了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, there's a lot of power here.
zh: 所以，这里面有很大的力量。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, talk about just from your advice to parents around boundaries and this idea of long-term resilience and then how that might translate.
zh: 那么，请谈谈你给家长关于界限的建议，以及这种长期抗逆力的理念，然后这可能会如何转化。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, I think related to being a sturdy leader, to make it more concrete, there's two jobs we have as a parent.
zh: 所以，我认为这与成为一个坚定的领导者有关，为了更具体一点，我们作为父母有两项工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think they're the same two jobs we have as any sturdy leader in any environment.
zh: 我认为这与我们在任何环境中作为一个坚定的领导者所拥有的两项工作是相同的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's setting boundaries, which are limits we set, certain decisions we make.
zh: 那就是设定界限，也就是我们设定的限制，我们要做出的某些决定。

speaker: Unknown
en: Boundaries often relate to your position of authority, where you have some long-term goal in mind that someone else in the system just isn't as aware of, or a two-year-old isn't as capable of holding onto, which is why you're in the position of making a decision.
zh: 界限通常与你的权威地位有关，你在心中有一个长期目标，而系统中的其他人并没有那么意识到，或者一个两岁的孩子没有那么能力去坚持，这就是为什么你处于做决定的位置。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, that's the boundaries.
zh: 所以，这就是界限。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the other side is, validating kids' or someone else's experience, while not being taken over by it.
zh: 另一方面是，确认孩子或他人的体验，而不被它接管。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think we've moved so far in this direction of validating kids' experience, and I'll go on record and saying, validating kids' feelings is an incomplete parenting approach.
zh: 我认为我们在确认孩子体验的这个方向上走得太多了，我要公开说，确认孩子的感觉是不完整的育儿方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is half of our job, but it doesn't work if we're not doing the other half, which is setting boundaries, which is the skill I feel best about teaching people, because I think it's what we're often lacking.
zh: 那是我们工作的一半，但如果我们不做另一半，即设定界限，它就不起作用，这是我教人时感觉最好的技能，因为我认为这是我们经常缺乏的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, my definition of boundaries, I do like because it's immediately usable and testable.
zh: 所以，我对界限的定义，我确实很喜欢，因为它立即可用且可测试。

speaker: Unknown
en: Boundaries are what you tell someone else you will do, and it requires the other person to do nothing.
zh: 界限是你告诉别人你会做什么，它要求对方什么都不做。

speaker: Unknown
en: Too often I hear people say, My kid doesn't respect my boundaries.
zh: 我经常听到人们说，我的孩子不尊重我的界限。

speaker: Unknown
en: My colleague doesn't respect my boundaries.
zh: 我的同事不尊重我的界限。

speaker: Unknown
en: And with all due respect, I often think, I think you have an incorrect definition of boundaries.
zh: 恕我直言，我经常认为，我认为你对界限的定义是不正确的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think you're probably making a request, which by the way, we do a lot.
zh: 我认为你可能是在提出要求，顺便说一下，我们经常这样做。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's nothing wrong with requests, but it's not a boundary.
zh: 要求没有任何问题，但它不是界限。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because if you're setting what you call a boundary and you're really making a request, you're giving all of your power away to the other person, because you're saying the success of this important moment depends on my two-year-old.
zh: 因为如果你在设定所谓的界限但你实际上是在提出要求，你就把你所有的力量都给了对方，因为你在说这个重要时刻的成功取决于我的两岁孩子。

speaker: Unknown
en: What?
zh: 什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm not going to give my power to my two-year-old.
zh: 我不会把我的力量给我的两岁孩子。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I always tell parents, I would bet on you any day over your kid, and it's not because I don't like your kid.
zh: 我总是告诉父母，我任何一天都会把你赌在你的孩子之上，这不是因为我不喜欢你的孩子。

speaker: Unknown
en: I do like your kid.
zh: 我确实喜欢你的孩子。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just believe in you more.
zh: 我只是更相信你。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so a good example of this, let's say, is, I have a kid, like I was describing before, who's not a people pleaser.
zh: 所以，一个很好的例子，比如，我有一个孩子，就像我之前描述的那样，不是一个讨人喜欢的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, I live in New York City, we live in a building with a lot of elevator buttons, where this kid, when he was younger, if he was left to his own devices, which had happened, he would press every elevator button, every one.
zh: 所以，我住在纽约市，我们住在一个有很多电梯按钮的大楼里，这个孩子，当他小时候，如果让他随心所欲，这发生过，他会按每一个电梯按钮，每一个。

speaker: Unknown
en: And with my other kids, one of them especially, I probably would've been able to make a request like, Hey, when we go on the elevator later, it's really important not to press all the buttons.
zh: 对于我的其他孩子，其中一个特别是，我可能能够提出一个要求，比如，嘿，当我们稍后坐电梯时，不按所有按钮真的很重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: Other people wait for this elevator, it's rude, whatever it is.
zh: 其他人等这部电梯，这很粗鲁，不管是什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's not a boundary.
zh: 那不是界限。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because I could say, Did I tell my kid what I will do?
zh: 因为我可以说，我告诉我的孩子我会做什么吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: No.
zh: 没有。

speaker: Unknown
en: Does it require my kid to do nothing?
zh: 它要求我的孩子什么都不做吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: No.
zh: 没有。

speaker: Unknown
en: A boundary, would be saying, when we go into the elevator, I'm going to stand between you and the button, sweetie, because I'm not going to let you press the buttons, because other people are waiting.
zh: 界限，会是说，当我们进入电梯时，我会站在你和按钮之间，亲爱的，因为我不会让你按那些按钮，因为其他人在等。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you have a kid like my kid, they're going to lunge for it anyway.
zh: 如果你有一个像我孩子那样的孩子，他们无论如何都会冲过去。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then I would block my kid, because I'm ready and I've done a workout, my muscles are ready.
zh: 然后我会挡住我的孩子，因为我准备好了，我已经做了锻炼，我的肌肉准备好了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm not going to let you do that.
zh: 我不会让你那样做。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, my kid might get upset, which allows me to do the other part of my jobs.
zh: 现在，我的孩子可能会心烦意乱，这让我可以做我工作的另一部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, I know you wish you could press those buttons or maybe just look kindly toward him and not say anything, but I'm going to still hold my boundary.
zh: 哦，我知道你希望你能按那些按钮，或者也许只是友善地看着他不说话，但我仍然会坚持我的界限。

speaker: Unknown
en: And in this example, I think one of the places we get into trouble as a parent, is we say, Don't press the buttons.
zh: 在这个例子中，我认为作为父母我们陷入麻烦的地方之一是，我们说，不要按按钮。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you press the buttons, no dessert tonight, or whatever, we threaten.
zh: 如果你按了按钮，今晚没有甜点，或者不管什么，我们威胁。

speaker: Unknown
en: But all of that is a way of completely undermining our authority, because I'm giving all of my power to my kid.
zh: 但所有这些都是在完全破坏我们的权威的一种方式，因为我在把我所有的力量给我的孩子。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm letting myself get frustrated.
zh: 我让自己感到沮丧。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, I'm taking away dessert, and the honest truth, if we want to be honest about it, is at night, I'm going to give my kids sorbet anyway and then make up something how sorbet isn't dessert, because it has fruit, all because I just didn't set a boundary in the first place.
zh: 现在，我拿走甜点，老实说，如果我们想诚实一点，就是晚上，反正我会给我的孩子冰淇淋，然后编造一些说法，说冰淇淋不是甜点，因为它有水果，这一切都仅仅是因为我一开始就没有设定界限。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think over and over, in parenting, maybe at work too, we're asking our kid to do our job for us, because we just don't want to deal with the emotional fallout, but that's what I call job confusion and makes everyone frustrated.
zh: 我认为一次又一次，在育儿中，也许在工作中也是，我们在要求我们的孩子为我们做我们的工作，因为我们只是不想处理情绪后果，这就是我所谓的职位混淆，这让每个人都感到沮丧。

speaker: Unknown
en: Your marketing website sets the tone for your brand and is the one touchpoint that every single one of your customer sees.
zh: 你的营销网站为你的品牌定下基调，是每一个客户看到的唯一接触点。

speaker: Unknown
en: In today's age, if you're still having a hard time making small changes and simple updates to it, you are doing something wrong.
zh: 在今天这个时代，如果你仍然很难对其进行微小的更改和简单的更新，那你一定是做错了什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is why so many companies from early stage startups to Fortune 500s, including companies like SpaceX, DoorDash, Zapier, Perplexity, and ElevenLabs turn to Framer, the website builder that turns your .com, from a formality into a tool for growth.
zh: 这就是为什么如此多的公司，从初创公司到财富 500 强，包括像 SpaceX、DoorDash、Zapier、Perplexity 和 ElevenLabs 这样的公司，都转向 Framer，这个网站构建器将你的 .com 从一种形式转变为增长工具。

speaker: Unknown
en: Framer works like your team's favorite design tool and comes with real-time collaboration, a robust CMS with everything you need for great SEO and advanced analytics that includes integrated A/B testing.
zh: Framer 的运作就像你团队最喜欢的设计工具，并配备了实时协作、一个功能强大的 CMS，拥有出色 SEO 所需的一切以及包含集成 A/B 测试的高级分析。

speaker: Unknown
en: Changes to your Framer site go live to the web in seconds, with a single click and without any help from engineering.
zh: 对你的 Framer 网站所做的更改只需点击一下，无需任何工程方面的帮助，即可在几秒钟内发布到网上。

speaker: Unknown
en: Whether you want to launch a new site, test a few...
zh: 无论你想推出一个新网站，测试几个...

speaker: Unknown
en: And without any help from engineering, whether you want to launch a new site, test a few landing pages, or migrate your full .com, Framer has programs for startups, scale-ups, and large enterprises to make going from idea to live site as easy and fast as possible.
zh: 而且无需任何工程方面的帮助，无论你想推出一个新网站、测试几个落地页，还是迁移你完整的 .com，Framer 都有针对初创公司、扩张型企业和大型企业的项目，让从想法到上线网站的过程尽可能简单和快速。

speaker: Unknown
en: Learn how to turn your website into a growth engine from a Framer expert, or just get started building for free today at framer.com/lenny.
zh: 从 Framer 专家那里学习如何将你的网站变成增长引擎，或者就在今天访问 framer.com/lenny 免费开始构建。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you're a Lenny's Product Pass subscriber, you get an entire year of Framer Pro for free.
zh: 如果你是 Lenny's Product Pass 的订阅者，你可以免费获得一整年的 Framer Pro。

speaker: Unknown
en: Check it out at framer.com/lenny.
zh: 访问 framer.com/lenny 查看详情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Rules and restrictions may apply.
zh: 可能适用规则和限制。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let me actually ask you a parenting question while we're on this topic as I'm trying to learn this boundaries thing better.
zh: 既然我们在谈论这个话题，让我实际上问你一个育儿问题，因为我正试图更好地学习这个界限问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: What's the correct way to, if they're not going to the car, and I'm like, "Okay.
zh: 如果他们不去车里，而我就像，“好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm going to carry you to the car if you don't do this."
zh: 如果你不这样做，我会把你抱到车里。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm still requesting them to go there.
zh: 我仍然在请求他们去那里。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is there a way to phrase that where they don't need to do anything?
zh: 有没有一种表达方式可以让他们不需要做任何事情？

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, boundaries are something I will do when they require my kid to do nothing, we want to try.
zh: 嗯，界限是当需要我的孩子什么都不做时我会做的事情，我们想尝试一下。

speaker: Unknown
en: Look, we have to get in the car and whatever I try.
zh: 看，我们必须上车，无论我尝试什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: I try a game, I try a song.
zh: 我尝试一个游戏，我尝试一首歌。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then again, the intention matters.
zh: 然后再一次，意图很重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: The reason I'm going to carry my kid to the car is actually, I don't want to let myself then be late and then yell at my kid and be frustrated.
zh: 我要把孩子抱到车里的原因其实是，我不想让自己迟到，然后对孩子大吼大叫并感到沮丧。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm doing it because I like my kid, and I'm on the same team as them.
