zh: 我这样做是因为我喜欢我的孩子，而且我和他们是一队的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So then I might say, "Look.
zh: 那么我可能会说，“看。

speaker: Unknown
en: It looks like it's hard for you to get to the car.
zh: 看来你去车里有点困难。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't really want to do this, but I don't know.
zh: 我真的不想这样做，但我不知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm going to turn around, I'm going to take a deep breath.
zh: 我要转过身，我要深吸一口气。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if by the time I turn around, you're still here, I'm going to pick you up.
zh: 而且如果当我转过身时，你还在这里，我会把你抱起来。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm going to carry you to the car.
zh: 我要把你抱到车里。

speaker: Unknown
en: And even if you're crying and kicking and screaming, I'm still going to do that, sweetie.
zh: 即使你在哭闹和尖叫，我仍然会这样做，亲爱的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I know sometimes it's hard to leave the house.
zh: 我知道有时很难出门。

speaker: Unknown
en: We do have to leave.
zh: 我们确实得走了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Here comes my deep breath."
zh: 我要深吸一口气了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, what's really important, I don't want to understate, is I think somewhere in us we think, "I'm going to do that, and then I'm going to be rewarded by my kid for my good parenting.
zh: 现在，真正重要的是，我不想低估的是，我认为在我们内心的某个地方我们认为，“我要那样做，然后我会因为我的良好育儿而得到孩子的回报。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm going to turn around and you're going to be like, 'I'm ready now, Dad.'"
zh: 我转过身，你会像，‘我准备好了，爸爸。’”

speaker: Unknown
en: That might happen in some households, but it actually won't reliably happen until you've actually set boundaries over and over, where it's not about taking advantage of you.
zh: 这可能发生在某些家庭中，但实际上，直到你真正一遍又一遍地设定界限，这才会可靠地发生，在那里这不关乎占你的便宜。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're just trying to learn the rules of the game.
zh: 他们只是想学习游戏规则。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so then I would pick up my kid.
zh: 那么我会抱起我的孩子。

speaker: Unknown
en: And by the way, depending on your kid's temperament, two out of the three of my kids would scream and cry.
zh: 顺便说一句，根据你孩子的气质，我的三个孩子中有两个会尖叫和哭泣。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then we tell ourselves, "Oh, I'm not a good parent.
zh: 然后我们告诉自己，“哦，我不是一个好父母。

speaker: Unknown
en: I messed that up."
zh: 我搞砸了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: No.
zh: 不。

speaker: Unknown
en: Again, a pilot is going to make a decision in certain key moments, not based on consensus.
zh: 再一次，飞行员会在某些关键时刻做出决定，而不是基于共识。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's say I'm flying to LA, and it's really turbulent, and the pilot says they have to make an emergency landing, and everyone's complaining, and they're like, "What?
zh: 假设我飞往洛杉矶，气流非常颠簸，飞行员说他们必须紧急降落，每个人都在抱怨，他们就像，“什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: It can't be that big of a deal.
zh: 没有那么严重吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know if that's a light.
zh: 我不知道那是不是一盏灯。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have a big meeting in LA."
zh: 我在洛杉矶有个重要的会议。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Can you imagine if the pilot was like, "Oh, Becky and Lenny are really upset about this.
zh: 你能想象如果飞行员就像，“哦，Becky 和 Lenny 对此非常不高兴。

speaker: Unknown
en: You know what, guys?
zh: 知道吗，伙计们？

speaker: Unknown
en: Forget it.
zh: 算了吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: Forget it.
zh: 算了吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: Forget the emergency landing."
zh: 忘了紧急降落吧。”

speaker: Unknown
en: What?
zh: 什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Even if we were upset, we feel deeply, deeply safe that our leader is doing something they believe is right with the information they have available.
zh: 即使我们很心烦，我们也感到深深的、深深的安全，因为我们的领导者正在根据他们掌握的信息做一些他们相信是正确的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: So even if I'm complaining when I get off the plane in Kansas, wherever I'm landing midway, I also in the back of my head am so grateful.
zh: 所以即使当我在堪萨斯下飞机时，或者无论我在中途降落哪里时我都在抱怨，我内心深处也非常感激。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I just want parents to know, when you set a true boundary, especially if it's new, your kid will tantrum and protest.
zh: 所以我只是想让父母们知道，当你设定一个真正的界限时，尤其是如果这是新事物，你的孩子会发脾气并抗议。

speaker: Unknown
en: But if you actually see that as a sign that you're successfully setting a boundary, instead of as a sign you did something wrong, your relationship with a tantrum changes.
zh: 但如果你实际上将其视为你成功设定界限的信号，而不是你做错事的信号，你与发脾气的这种关系就会改变。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're almost oddly sickly grateful for a sign that you set a boundary.
zh: 你甚至会莫名其妙地病态地感激这样一个你设定了界限的信号。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you're like, "Oh, this is actually..." I always say to myself, "This is going according to plan."
zh: 你会像这样，“噢，这实际上是……”我总是对自己说，“这一切正按计划进行。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And it makes the moment so much easier.
zh: 这让这一刻容易得多。

speaker: Unknown
en: This advice you give about being the leader is really important, and I could see how it connects deeply, too, in the work environment.
zh: 你给出的关于成为领导者的建议非常重要，我也能看到它如何在工作环境中深深相连。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm curious just if there's more advice there.
zh: 我只是好奇那里是否还有更多建议。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the idea that I've been hearing is just, people want you to be their leader, both as a kid and in the work environment.
zh: 所以我一直在听到的观点就是，人们希望你成为他们的领导者，无论是作为孩子还是在工作环境中。

speaker: Unknown
en: They don't want you to be like, "Okay, what does everyone think?
zh: 他们不希望你像这样，“好的，大家怎么想？

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's vote."
zh: 我们投票吧。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Talk about just the insight there.
zh: 谈谈那里的见解吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: First of all, I think a lot of things, two things are true.
zh: 首先，我认为很多事情，两件事都是真的。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's definitely a time for consensus.
zh: 肯定有达成共识的时候。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a time for getting 100%.
zh: 有获得 100% 认同的时候。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's a really important part of leadership, too.
zh: 这也是领导力非常重要的一部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: But inherently, you're in a position of leadership because you have experience other people don't have, you have insight, you have judgment.
zh: 但本质上，你处于领导职位是因为你有别人没有的经验，你有洞察力，你有判断力。

speaker: Unknown
en: You might just very literally have access to company information and data that other people don't have.
zh: 你可能真的只是能够接触到其他人没有的公司信息和数据。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're not all working from the same set of information.
zh: 你们并不是都在基于同一组信息工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, there's definitely a time for consensus building, but at least in parenting, I think we've over corrected.
zh: 所以，绝对有建立共识的时候，但至少在育儿方面，我认为我们矫枉过正了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I need to collect more data from the working world if we've over corrected.
zh: 如果我们也矫枉过正了，我需要从工作世界中收集更多数据。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it feels really good for people to hear some version of, "I took in everything you said, your voices matter, and I want to share a decision I've made with all the information I have.
zh: 我认为听到某种版本的“我接受了你说的所有话，你们的声音很重要，我想根据我掌握的所有信息分享我做出的决定”，这对人们来说感觉真的很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: It might not make sense to all of you at the time.
zh: 这在当时可能对你们所有人来说都没有意义。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can come talk to me separately.
zh: 你可以私下单独来找我谈。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I have conviction that this is the next thing we're going to experiment with," whatever the decision is."
zh: 我坚信这是我们接下来要实验的事情，”无论决定是什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: I said this word earlier, and it's another word I say that maybe other people don't say.
zh: 我之前说过这个词，这也是我会说的另一个词，也许其他人不会说。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm always drawn to people I can locate.
zh: 我总是被我能定位的人所吸引。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the word.
zh: 就是这个词。

speaker: Unknown
en: I can locate you.
zh: 我能定位你。

speaker: Unknown
en: I know what you feel.
zh: 我知道你的感受。

speaker: Unknown
en: I know your POV.
zh: 我知道你的观点。

speaker: Unknown
en: I personally love people I can locate even if I really disagree with them more than being around people who seem to mirror what I'm saying.
zh: 就我个人而言，我喜欢我能定位的人，即使我真的不同意他们，也胜过和那些似乎只是附和我所说的话的人在一起。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've actually learned a lot in a working environment that's what I need because I have a lot of strong ideas.
zh: 我实际上在工作环境中学到了很多，那就是我所需要的，因为我有很多强烈的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I love people who push back and have a POV.
zh: 所以我喜欢那些有反驳意见并有观点的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think especially at work when there's so much going on, it could be so stressful, having a leader you can locate, which means I have a POV to be able to say, "This might not be what you want.
zh: 我认为尤其是在工作中，当有这么多事情发生时，可能会非常有压力，拥有一个你能定位的领导者，这意味着我有观点能够说，“这可能不是你想要的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I believe this is the best decision, and I believe we're a strong enough team to work toward that."
zh: 我相信这是最好的决定，我相信我们要足够强大的团队去为此努力。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that's very, very holding, not all the time, but in more situations than we might realize.
zh: 我认为这是非常非常令人安心的，不是所有时候，而是在比我们意识到的更多的情况下。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the advice here is, even if it may not feel like your kid wants you to just tell them what to do or your team wants to have opinions and have a big say, in reality, the human brain seeks somebody to lead them.
zh: 所以这里的建议是，即使感觉上不像你的孩子想让你只是告诉他们该做什么，或者你的团队想发表意见并有很大的发言权，但实际上，人类的大脑寻求某人来领导他们。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that's exactly right.
zh: 我认为那是完全正确的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think we can all think back on our life.
zh: 我认为我们都可以回顾我们的一生。

speaker: Unknown
en: And again, you rarely say thank you to these people, but if you think back on really hard moments, or I don't know, if I was out of control at some party, and I don't know, I was being mean to people, I was saying awful things.
zh: 同样，你很少对这些说谢谢，但如果你回顾真正艰难的时刻，或者我不知道，如果我在某个聚会上失控了，我不知道，我当时对人们刻薄，我说的那些可怕的话。

speaker: Unknown
en: Who knows?
zh: 谁知道呢？

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe someone's drunk.
zh: 也许有人喝醉了。

speaker: Unknown
en: An act of love in that moment would be, at least if it was me and my husband, picking me up and carrying me out, kind of like, "I'm not going to let you self-destruct."
zh: 在那一刻，爱的行为至少对我来说，如果是我的丈夫的话，就是把我抱起来带我出去，有点像，“我不会让你自我毁灭。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And if I was like, "No, I'm fine," I might even look back later and think, "Why did you let me make that decision?"
zh: 而如果我说，“不，我很好”，我甚至可能事后回想并认为，“你为什么让我做那个决定？”

speaker: Unknown
en: I'll share a story, because some of this comes from it.
zh: 我会分享一个故事，因为其中一些内容来源于此。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think about this teenager I was working with in private practice and she came to me, I'm just doing a first session, "Okay, tell me what to bring you here."
zh: 我想到这个我曾在私人执业中一起工作的青少年，她来到我这里，我只是做第一次面谈，“好的，告诉我是什么带你来这里。”

speaker: Unknown
en: She's like, "Well, you can see on my arms, I'm cutting."
zh: 她说，“嗯，你可以看到我的手臂上，我在自残。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I was like, "Okay, keep going."
zh: 我就像，“好吧，继续。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I was like, "Well, how long have you been doing that? "
zh: 我就像，“嗯，你这样做多久了？”

speaker: Unknown
en: She goes, "Two years."
zh: 她说，“两年。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I said, "You just said I'm the first therapist you've ever seen.
zh: 我说，“你刚才是说我是你见过的第一个治疗师。

speaker: Unknown
en: So what's gone on in the last two years?"
zh: 那过去两年发生了什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: She was so snarky.
zh: 她非常刻薄。

speaker: Unknown
en: And she goes, "Oh, wow.
zh: 然后她说，“噢，哇。

speaker: Unknown
en: My parents tried me to send me to a therapist two years ago, but I said to them, "Oh, so you think I'm the messed up one in the family, and you can send me.
zh: 两年前我父母想送我去治疗师那里，但我对他们说，‘噢，所以你们觉得我是家里那个坏掉的人，你们可以把我送去。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm just going to lie the whole time and waste your money."
zh: 我就一直撒谎，浪费你们的钱。’”

speaker: Unknown
en: My heart always races when I think about this moment.
zh: 每当我想到这一刻，我的心就会狂跳。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's so visceral still.
zh: 它仍然如此发自肺腑。

speaker: Unknown
en: And something in me, I don't know, I was on my game that day.
zh: 在我内心深处，我不知道，那天我表现得很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was like, "Becky, just be quiet.
zh: 我就像，“Becky，闭嘴。

speaker: Unknown
en: Don't say anything."
zh: 什么都别说。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I don't know, 10, 15 seconds later, everything changed for her.
zh: 我不知道，10到15秒后，一切都为她改变了。

speaker: Unknown
en: She looked down, and then she looked up, and she just goes, "Can you believe they let me make that decision?"
zh: 她低下头，然后抬起头，她就说，“你能相信他们让我做那个决定吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: She felt so betrayed and so not taken care of.
zh: 她感到如此被背叛，如此不被照顾。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it just made me realize, at our worst moments, first of all, at our worst moments we speak, I think, our fears, not our wishes, Number One.
zh: 这让我意识到，在我们最糟糕的时刻，首先，在我们最糟糕的时刻我们说出的，我认为，是我们的恐惧，而不是我们的愿望，这是第一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: And at our worst moments, we're not a good place to make good decisions for ourselves.
zh: 在我们最糟糕的时刻，我们并不是处于能为自己做出正确决定的好状态。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we don't want to be punished, but sometimes it is an act of love.
zh: 我们不想受到惩罚，但这有时是一种爱的行为。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I tell this to people very explicitly when they're like, "I know my teenager needs to go to therapy, but they're refusing."
zh: 当人们说，“我知道我的青少年需要去接受治疗，但他们拒绝”时，我非常明确地告诉他们这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'll give them an exact script, "Here's what you're going to say."
zh: 我会给他们一个确切的脚本，“这是你要说的话。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's some version of, "Look, I am going to drive you every Thursday to therapy.
zh: 这话大概是，“听着，我要在每个周四开车送你去治疗。

speaker: Unknown
en: Whatever you do in the room, whether you lie, whether you're silent, that's not on me.
zh: 你在房间里做什么，无论你是撒谎还是沉默，那不关我的事。

speaker: Unknown
en: But my Number One job is making decisions that I think are good for you even if you're not happy.
zh: 但我的首要工作是做出我认为对你有益的决定，即使你不高兴。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's how much I care about you.
zh: 这就是我有多在乎你。

speaker: Unknown
en: And this is one of those times."
zh: 而这就是那些时刻之一。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Every parent who told me they'd done that, their kids on some level, they're like, "I needed that."
zh: 每一个告诉我他们那样做了的父母，他们的孩子某种程度上，他们会说，“我需要那个。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think the equivalent in the workplace is, there are moments when things are really turbulent, when people are, they're saying different things because they're anxious, and they're looking for a leader to kind of say, "Hey, I've heard this all.
zh: 我认为这在职场上对等的情况是，有些时刻事情真的非常动荡，当人们很焦虑时说着不同的事情，他们在寻找一个领导者某种程度上说，“嘿，我都听过了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Here's what I'm doing."
zh: 这就是我要做的。”

speaker: Unknown
en: You might even say, "I'm not going to lie to you.
zh: 你甚至可以说，“我不会对你撒谎。

speaker: Unknown
en: Do I have complete conviction that this is right?
zh: 我对这个是正确的有完全的信念吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: No.
zh: 没有。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I've put it all together, and this is what we're going with, and I feel good about this direction.
zh: 但我把所有事情综合起来，这就是我们要采取的方案，我对这个方向感觉良好。

speaker: Unknown
en: We'll reassess in a month.
zh: 我们会在一个月后重新评估。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's go do this, team."
zh: 让我们去做吧，团队。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think everyone is waiting in certain moments for someone to show up that way.
zh: 我认为在某些时刻每个人都在等待有人这样出现。

speaker: Unknown
en: That was an awesome story.
zh: 那是个很棒的故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: It makes me think about it, just people, they will take what they can get, and they'll be wondering like, "Hey, wow, I'm getting away with all this."
zh: 它让我思考这一点，就是人们，他们会接受能得到的，他们会想，“嘿，哇，我能逃避这一切。”

speaker: Unknown
en: When will they realize that someone working at a company where they're not working that hard, "I'll just get away with this as long as I can until they tell me that I can't."
zh: 他们什么时候会意识到在一个公司工作的人并没有那么努力工作，“我会只要能逃避就逃避，直到他们告诉我不能。”

speaker: Dr. Becky Kennedy
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I just saw the show on Broadway called Punch.
zh: 我刚在百老汇看了一个叫 Punch 的演出。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's this really interesting story about this guy, true story that it's based on, where essentially he ended up punching someone at a bar, the guy falls and dies, but he ends up getting to know that person's parents after incarceration.
zh: 这是一个关于这个人的非常有趣的故事，基于真实故事，基本上他在酒吧里打了某人一拳，那人摔倒并死了，但他在监禁后最终认识了那个人的父母。

speaker: Unknown
en: And one of the things that comes up, because I think we tell ourselves, people will take advantage, they'll take all the space you give them.
zh: 其中出现的一件事是，因为我认为我们告诉自己，人们会利用，他们会占据你给他们的所有空间。

speaker: Unknown
en: I do tend to think about it in a more of an MGI way, where what happened in this show is, these parents, this guy killed their son.
zh: 我确实倾向于用更 MGI 的方式来思考，在这个节目中发生的事情是，这些父母，这个家伙杀了他们的儿子。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the question they come to him with, because they end up meeting is, "Well, tell us what you're going to do with your life here.
zh: 他们向他的提问是，因为他们最终见面了，“嗯，告诉我们你余生打算做什么。 [[746]**] 你现在余生打算做什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: What are you going to do with your life now?"
zh: 你现在的人生打算做什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: And he ends up saying, nobody had ever asked me that question.
zh: 他最终说，从来没人问过我这个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: Nobody had believed in me.
zh: 从来没有人相信过我。

speaker: Unknown
en: Nobody had ever believed I could do anything useful.
zh: 从来没有人相信我能做任何有用的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Changed the course of his life.
zh: 改变了他的人生轨迹。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a crazy story.
zh: 这是一个疯狂的故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I think about at work when someone, yeah, whatever it is we think they're getting away with, or kids, sometimes I think kids are asking, "Is there an adult here?
zh: 但我在工作中会想到，当某人，是的，无论我们认为他们在做什么，还是孩子们，有时我认为孩子们在问，“这里有成年人吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is there an adult here who will help me, who sees that this is my form of acting out and being out of control?
zh: 这里有愿意帮我的成年人吗，能看到这是我的一种发泄和失控的表现吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And will somebody help me?"
zh: 会有人帮我吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And you know why kids act out more?
zh: 你知道为什么孩子们会更经常地发泄吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not because they're taking advantage of you.
zh: 不是因为他们在利用你。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's because they feel that much more dysregulated because they don't feel like there's an adult in the room who's willing to put a container on their shell-less egg to help them come back together and move forward in a better direction.
zh: 是因为他们感到更加失调，因为他们觉得房间里没有一个成年人愿意在他们没有壳的蛋上放一个容器来帮助他们重新聚拢并向更好的方向前进。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think about just people that don't have parents that are there and always seek someone as their partner that's more, I don't know, that gives them that later.
zh: 我只是想到那些没有父母在身边的人，总是寻找某人作为伴侣，那个更，我不知道，那个后来给了他们那个的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: This connects really well with this idea of building resilience long-term versus short-term.
zh: 这与建立长期韧性而非短期应对的想法联系得很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think about in the work environment, doing the hard thing, you will be better off versus just letting things progress because it's hard for them to do.
zh: 在工作环境中，我想的是做困难的事，你会过得更好，而不是仅仅让事情顺其自然，因为这对他们来说很难。

speaker: Unknown
en: You think people will not want to hear bad news.
zh: 你会认为人们不想听到坏消息。

speaker: Unknown
en: Talk about just that kind of lesson and how that might translate.
zh: 谈谈这种教训以及它可能如何转化。

speaker: Unknown
en: One of the chapters in my book and something that guides our whole parenting philosophy is just codified as resilience over happiness.
zh: 我书中的一个章节，也是指导我们整个育儿理念的东西，被编纂为“韧性优于快乐”。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I don't know if you hear this, Lenny, but I used to hear this all the time and I couldn't let myself let this small talk moment go.
zh: Lenny，我不知道你是否听过这个，但我以前总是听到这个，我无法让自己放过这种闲聊的时刻。

speaker: Unknown
en: "Don't you just want your kid to be happy?
zh: “你不只是想让你的孩子快乐吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: You just want your kid to be happy, right?"
zh: 你只是想让你的孩子快乐，对吧？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'd always ruin a perfectly harmless comment.
zh: 我总是会把一个完全无伤大雅的评论搞砸。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was like, "No.
zh: 我会说，“不。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then again, we think in extremes, "Oh, you want your kid to be unhappy?"
zh: 然后又一次，我们极端地思考，“哦，你想让你的孩子不快乐？”

speaker: Unknown
en: "No, I definitely don't want my kid to be unhappy."
zh: “不，我绝对不想让我的孩子不快乐。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But I think if we zoom out, especially in childhood that I think I can make a parallel to work, too, optimizing for happiness in childhood is the quickest way to build anxiety and fragility in adulthood.
zh: 但我认为如果我们拉远视角，特别是在童年，我想我也可以把它与工作类比，在童年时期优化快乐是在成年期建立焦虑和脆弱的最快方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: Hard stop.
zh: 停止。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because in a kid's early years, what they're really learning is, "What range of experiences am I able to cope with?
zh: 因为在孩子成长的早期，他们真正学习的是，“我能应对多大范围的经历？

speaker: Unknown
en: In how many different situations can I find my capability?
zh: 在多少种不同的情况下我能发现我的能力？

speaker: Unknown
en: Can I feel like I can get something done?
zh: 我能觉得我能把事情做成吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Can I get through something?
zh: 我能挺过什么事吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Or do I believe that the only way to feel like myself is to bring every form of discomfort to a zero and only find happiness?"
zh: 还是我相信找回自我的唯一方法是将所有形式的不适感归零，只寻找快乐？”

speaker: Unknown
en: The irony is, in adulthood, the thing that clouds out happiness often is our inability to manage disappointment, jealousy.
zh: 讽刺的是，在成年期，经常掩盖快乐的事情是我们无法管理失望、嫉妒。

speaker: Unknown
en: Those things will always overpower happiness.
zh: 那些东西总是会压倒快乐。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so happiness actually comes in adulthood from our ability to manage the widest range of difficult situations.
zh: 所以成年期的快乐实际上来自于我们管理最广泛范围的困难情况的能力。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's what resilience is.
zh: 这就是韧性。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's our ability to handle the widest range of experiences.
zh: 这是我们处理最广泛经历的能力。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm not talking about toxic, but just disappointment, jealousy, anger, feeling less than.
zh: 我说的不是有毒的事情，只是失望、嫉妒、愤怒、感到自卑。

speaker: Unknown
en: These are just human experiences we have.
zh: 这些只是我们拥有的人类经历。

speaker: Unknown
en: And unintentionally, something's happened over the generations where we're optimizing for kids' happiness.
zh: 而且无意中，几代人之间发生了一些事情，我们在为孩子优化快乐。

speaker: Unknown
en: Our kids will say something like, "I'm the only one in my class who can't read," and we hop in with, "Oh, that's not true," or, "But you're so good at soccer, but you play chess, but you're doing this," as if I can't even tolerate my kids' upset feelings.
zh: 我们的孩子会说类似这样的话，“我是班上唯一一个不会阅读的人”，而我们跳进去说，“哦，那不是真的”，或者，“但是你足球踢得很好，但是你下棋，但是你在做这个”，好像我甚至不能容忍我孩子的难过情绪。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I tell parents all the time, our kids can only learn to tolerate the feelings we tolerate in them.
zh: 我总是告诉父母，我们的孩子只能学会容忍我们在他们身上容忍的感觉。

speaker: Unknown
en: Nothing scarier to a kid that when they're upset, if I can't tolerate their upset feelings, that makes them doubly upset because it's like, "Oh, I guess this is unmanageable," which leads to adulthood where anything but comfortable and happy and doing a perfect job is actually experienced in your body is even worse, because you spent that many years thinking those feelings were wrong.
zh: 对孩子来说没有什么比这更可怕的了，当他们难过时，如果我不能容忍他们的难过情绪，那会让他们加倍难过，因为就像，“哦，我猜这是无法管理的”，这导致了成年期，除了舒适和快乐以及做一份完美的工作以外的任何东西，实际上在你的身体里体验到的更糟，因为你花了那么多年认为那些感觉是错误的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So what that means is, when our kids are young and they go through hard experiences, those are our best opportunities to wire them for resilience and ironically for happiness later on.
zh: 所以这意味着，当我们的孩子还小，他们经历困难的事情时，这些是我们为他们建立韧性——以及讽刺的是，为了他们以后的快乐——布线的最佳机会。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because if I can support my kid, I'm not going to say, "Uh, no big deal," but there's a lot between no big deal and let me call the school to make this all better for you.
zh: 因为如果我能支持我的孩子，我不会说，“呃，没什么大不了的”，但在“没什么大不了”和“让我给学校打电话把这一切都为你搞定”之间有很多东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a whole world.
zh: 这是一个完整的世界。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the world I think we try to live in and good inside where I can help my kids see they're capable of dealing with this.
zh: 我认为我们在 Good Inside 努力生活的就是那个世界，在那里我可以帮助我的孩子看到他们有能力处理这件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's going to help them so much more over the years.
zh: 这在未来的岁月里会极大地帮助他们。

speaker: Unknown
en: How do you think that translates to work?
zh: 你认为这如何转化到工作中？

speaker: Unknown
en: The way I think about it is just like, it's okay to upset people.
zh: 我认为这件事的方式就像，让人心烦是可以的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're better off.
zh: 你会过得更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it was Kim Scott from Radical Candor had this great story where she had someone she was working with, he was doing a bad job.
zh: 我想是《 Radical Candor 》（彻底坦诚）的 Kim Scott 有这个很棒的故事，她有一个和她一起工作的人，他工作做得不好。

speaker: Unknown
en: She didn't want to upset him.
zh: 她不想让他心烦。

speaker: Unknown
en: She didn't give him any feedback.
zh: 她没有给他任何反馈。

speaker: Unknown
en: Nine months later, he just didn't get any better, so she had to fire him.
zh: 九个月后，他就是没有好转，所以她不得不解雇了他。

speaker: Unknown
en: And he's like, "Why didn't you tell me that I wasn't doing what you wanted me to do?
zh: 然后他说：“你为什么不告诉我我没有做你想要我做的事？”

speaker: Unknown
en: You could have told me somewhere along the way."
zh: 你本来可以在这个过程中告诉我的。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And to us, it feels like, "I don't want to hurt your feelings."
zh: 对我们来说，感觉就像，“我不想伤害你的感情。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But then it ends up hurting their feelings a lot worse.
zh: 但那样最终会更严重地伤害他们的感情。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a lot worse for them.
zh: 对他们来说情况更糟。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think there's so many parallels.
zh: 我认为有很多相似之处。

speaker: Unknown
en: One of the reasons I love talking about childhood is because that's when our brain is wiring.
zh: 我喜欢谈论童年的原因之一是因为那是我们大脑布线的时候。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's when we form the blueprint, or my friend Maile calls it the factory settings, for the rest of our kids' lives.
zh: 那是我们形成蓝图的时候，或者我的朋友 Maile 把它称为出厂设置，为了我们的孩子余下的生活。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the ultimate, is to wire them with the default that works for them.
zh: 最终目标是用对他们有效的默认设置来给他们布线。

speaker: Unknown
en: But actually you can think about work relationships the same way, where of course people come, their brain's already wired, they're older, or somewhat.
zh: 但实际上你可以用同样的方式考虑工作关系，当然人会进来，他们的大脑已经布好线了，他们更老了，或者稍微老一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: But if you think about it as a building, your first number of months with someone is, you're building the foundation for the whole building, which is the relationship you're going to have.
zh: 但如果你把它想象成一座建筑，你与某人相处的前几个月，你正在为整座建筑打地基，也就是你们将要建立的关系。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the earlier you're just optimizing for comfort and happiness, the more fragile your building's going to be.
zh: 而且你越早在初期只是追求舒适和快乐，你的建筑就会越脆弱。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you're right.
zh: 你是对的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Then you get to six months and you're like, "Oh, this person isn't doing so well.
zh: 然后到了六个月，你会想，“噢，这个人表现不太好。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm going to give them feedback."
zh: 我要给他们反馈。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But in their mind, they're thinking, "I've never gotten a piece of constructively critical feedback since I've been here.
zh: 但在他们脑海中，他们在想，“自从我来这里以来，我从未得到过任何建设性的批评反馈。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I thought the nature of my relationship in this workplace was one of butterflies and rainbows all the time."
zh: 所以我以为我在这个工作场所的人际关系的本质一直都是蝴蝶和彩虹。”

speaker: Unknown
en: That doesn't mean Day One I think you should sit someone down and say like, "Here's everything you're doing wrong."
zh: 这并不意味着我认为第一天你应该让某人坐下来然后说，“这是你做错的所有事情。”

speaker: Unknown
en: They're like, "I just started my job."
zh: 他们会想，“我才刚开始工作。”

speaker: Unknown
en: There's obviously reasonableness.
zh: 显然有个合理性标准。

speaker: Unknown
en: But yes, when we're thinking about a resilient work culture, we want people who can say, "This is hard and I can do hard things," not, "This is hard, so someone must be at fault and it should be easier."
zh: 但是的，当我们考虑一种有韧性的工作文化时，我们想要能够说，“这很难，但我能做困难的事”的人，而不是“这很难，所以肯定有人错了，而且这应该更容易。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I don't know anyone who would argue for the second culture.
zh: 而且我不认识任何会为第二种文化辩护的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think we should ask ourselves, "Well, what am I doing in the nature of my conversations with people that help me build toward that?
zh: 所以我认为我们应该问自己，“好吧，我在与人交谈的本质中做了什么来帮助我建立那种关系？

speaker: Unknown
en: I'll make it really concrete, because I think so much of dealing with anxiety and struggle, not all of it, but I like a good formula, is this combination of, "I believe you and I believe in you."
zh: 我会把它讲得非常具体，因为我认为处理焦虑和挣扎的很大一部分，不是全部，但我喜欢一个公式，就是这种结合，“我相信你，我也相信你。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I think one of the ways we've majorly misunderstood anxiety, whether at any age, is we're doing the I believe you part, but we've forgotten the I believe in you part, and they're both, again, really necessary together, which might sound like this.
zh: 我认为我们严重误解焦虑的方式之一，无论在什么年龄，都是我们在做“我相信你”这一部分，但我们忘记了“我相信你”这一部分，而且它们两个，再次强调，真的需要结合在一起，这听起来可能像这样。

speaker: Unknown
en: "Look, I believe you.
zh: “听着，我相信你。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is a hard project.
zh: 这是一个很难的项目。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're totally right.
zh: 你完全正确。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you've never done something like this before.
zh: 而且你以前从未做过这样的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: It makes sense," that's such a good phrase, "It makes sense you're nervous about it."
zh: 这很合理，”这是一个非常好的短语，“你对此感到紧张是很合理的。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Or something I say to my kids that I should say more at work.
zh: 或者我会对我孩子说的话，也是我应该在工作中多说的。

speaker: Unknown
en: "I'd be nervous if you weren't nervous.
zh: “如果你不紧张，我反而会紧张。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have a big task.
zh: 你有一项艰巨的任务。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'd be nervous if you weren't nervous.
zh: 如果你不紧张，我反而会紧张。

speaker: Unknown
en: Of course you're nervous.
zh: 你当然会紧张。

speaker: Unknown
en: That makes total sense.
zh: 这完全合理。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'd feel the same way.
zh: 我也会有同样的感觉。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the reason we think you can take on this project is because we know you can figure this out.
zh: 我们认为你能承担这个项目的原因是因为我们知道你能解决这个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: By the way, I'm here to answer questions and help you along the way, but I just want to make sure you hear that from me.
zh: 顺便说一下，我在这里是为了回答问题并沿途帮助你，但我只是想确保你能从我这里听到这句话。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes, this is hard and I also know you can work through this.
zh: 是的，这很难，而且我也知道你能搞定这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the way you're going to feel at the end, you're going to feel so proud of yourself.
zh: 你最后的感觉，你会为自己感到非常骄傲。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I don't want to take away the project from you, because I kind of think I'm taking away that feeling.
zh: 而且我不想从这个项目中把你撤下来，因为我有点认为我是在拿走那种感觉。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's going to be the feeling that propels you to do all the other amazing things you want to do here.
zh: 而那种感觉将是推动你在这里做所有其他你想做的令人惊叹的事情的动力。

speaker: Unknown
en: And let's work toward that."
zh: 让我们为此努力吧。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's actually, Lenny, something I used to say to my kids when they were your kids' age.
zh: 实际上，Lenny，这是我过去常在我孩子像你孩子那么大的时候对他们说的话。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just distinctly remember this time one of my kids was whining about this puzzle, and he's like, "Do it for me.
zh: 我清楚地记得有一次我的一个孩子在抱怨这个拼图，他说，“帮我做。

speaker: Unknown
en: Finish it for me.
zh: 帮我完成它。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have to do it."
zh: 你必须做。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And of course, the easiest thing to shut down the tantrum is like, "Fine, here's how you do it."
zh: 当然，平息发脾气最简单的方法就是，“好吧，这做的方法是这样的。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But it was the day I had a little more bandwidth.
zh: 但那是我稍微有点空闲时间的一天。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I heard myself sing something that, I don't know, it felt like healing to me, too.
zh: 我听到我自己唱了点什么，我不知道，这感觉对我来说也像是一种治愈。

speaker: Unknown
en: Honestly, I said, "Look, you're right, this is a hard puzzle.
zh: 老实说，我说：“听着，你是对的，这是一个很难的谜题。

speaker: Unknown
en: It makes sense you're frustrated.
zh: 你感到沮丧是合理的。

speaker: Unknown
en: All of us feel frustrated when we want to do something and haven't figured it out yet.
zh: 当我们想做某事却还没想通时，我们都会感到沮丧。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're feeling this moment right.
zh: 你对当下的这种感觉是对的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Take a break, take a breath, but I want to tell you why I'm not going to do this for you.
zh: 休息一下，深呼吸，但我想告诉你为什么我不会替你做这件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's no better feeling in the world than watching yourself work on something and make progress on something and maybe even complete something that you originally didn't think you could do.
zh: 这个世界上没有比看着自己努力做某事、在某事上取得进步，甚至完成某件你原本认为自己做不到的事更好的感觉了。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's literally no better feeling than that, and I will not take that feeling away from you."
zh: 字面上没有比那更好的感觉了，我不会从你那里夺走那种感觉。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think in the workplace, and definitely in parenting, we should be mindful of how often we take feelings of capability away from our kid.
zh: 我认为在工作场所，当然在育儿中，我们应该要注意我们有多频繁地从孩子那里夺走能力感。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I take that very seriously, because I feel very passionate about building a world where kids become adults who feel capable.
zh: 我非常认真地对待这一点，因为我对于建立一个孩子们能成长为有能力的成年人的世界充满热情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think we can do that in these small moments more often.
zh: 我认为我们可以更经常在这些小时刻中做到这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: As you share these stories and examples, I'm just like, "I wish I had Dr Becky in my ear constantly giving me advice as I'm doing this stuff."
zh: 当你分享这些故事和例子时，我就像，“我希望我在做这些事情时 Dr Becky 一直在我的耳边给我建议。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And we'll talk about how people sort of can get that.
zh: 我们会谈谈人们如何能获得那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I'm actually really happy you talked about this I believe you Lesson.
zh: 但我真的很高兴你谈到了这个“我相信你”这一课。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was going to bring it up.
zh: 我正打算提这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: That was right next on my notes.
zh: 那就在我的笔记上的下一条。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because I've used it with my wife, actually, and it is so good and so effective.
zh: 因为我已经和我的妻子用过它了，实际上，它真的非常好，非常有效。

speaker: Unknown
en: And this is why I love your advice is that it works not just for kids, it works for adults.
zh: 这就是为什么我喜欢你的建议，就是它不仅对孩子有效，对成年人也有效。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just like, "I believe you.
zh: 就像是，“我相信你。

speaker: Unknown
en: I believe you feel this, even though I don't know why and I wouldn't feel that way, but just I believe you."
zh: 我相信你有这种感觉，尽管我不知道为什么，我也不会那样觉得，但只是我相信你。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I like this, this makes sense piece.
zh: 我喜欢这个，“这讲得通”这一部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: I haven't thought about that.
zh: 我没想过那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then the I believe in you, that's interesting because I did talk about, the way I remember phrasing this, this was a moment I really remember just like, "You're smart, you'll figure this out."
zh: 然后那个“我相信你”，这很有趣，因为我确实谈到过，我记得我当时的措辞，那一刻我真的记得就像，“你很聪明，你会想明白的。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And then she just went back to me and she's like, "Wait.
zh: 然后她回到我身边，她就像，“等等。

speaker: Unknown
en: What did you just do there?"
zh: 你刚刚在那里做了什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: That felt really good.
zh: 那感觉真的很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, if you think about, and I'm very visual, so if you think about someone struggling, picture them in a hole, not an abyss, just like a little hole, okay?
zh: 好吧，如果你想一想，而且我是很视觉化的，所以如果你想象某人在挣扎，想象他们在在一个洞里，不是深渊，就像一个小洞，好吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And we're a relational species, at least we'll see what happens with AI, but for now we're a relational species, okay?
zh: 而且我们是一个关系型物种，至少我们会看看 AI 会发生什么，但目前我们是一个关系型物种，好吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And so when we're really struggling, inherently, the reason why we're struggling is we're in a hole and we can't see the way out of it.
zh: 所以当我们真的在挣扎时，本质上，我们挣扎的原因是我们在一个洞里，我们看不见出路。

speaker: Unknown
en: If we were in a hole and could see the way out, we wouldn't struggle.
zh: 如果我们在一个洞里并且能看到出路，我们就不会挣扎。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the only reason something feels so hard is because you're in a hole without visibility.
zh: 所以某事感觉这么难的唯一原因是因为你在一个没有可见度的洞里。

speaker: Unknown
en: So what do you need as a relational species?
zh: 那么作为一个关系型物种你需要什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: You need someone you trust to have one foot in the hole with you.
zh: 你需要一个你信任的人和你一起在洞里放一只脚。

speaker: Unknown
en: You do.
zh: 你需要。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the I believe you part.
zh: 那就是我相信你那一部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it might be saying to your kid, and those words are so powerful.
zh: 所以可能是对你的孩子说，而且那些话是如此有力。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think deep down, Lenny, that's literally what we're all looking for as humans is just to be believed.
zh: 我认为在内心深处，Lenny，那实际上就是我们人类都在寻找的，就是被相信。

speaker: Unknown
en: "I believe you.
zh: “我相信你。

speaker: Unknown
en: You really don't want to go to soccer practice.
zh: 你真的不想去参加足球训练。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're no longer starting on the team.
zh: 你不再是首发了。

speaker: Unknown
en: The first soccer practice after you lose your starting spot sucks, totally sucks.
zh: 在你失去首发位置后的第一次足球训练糟透了，完全糟透了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I believe you."
zh: 我相信你。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But if you think about them in that hole, they need you to have one foot out of the hole.
zh: 但如果你想象他们在那个洞里，他们需要你有一只脚在洞外。

speaker: Unknown
en: They need one foot in, but they need one foot out.
zh: 他们需要一只脚在里面，但他们需要一只脚在外面。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because if we can't see a more capable version of our kid than they can access in that moment, we've collapsed into the hole with them.
zh: 因为如果我们无法看到比我们的孩子在那个时刻能接触到的更有能力的版本，我们就已经和他们一起塌陷到洞里了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so the I believe you is, I believe in you would be, "Look, you're going to go to practice today.
zh: 所以我相信你就是，我相信你会是，“听着，你今天要去参加训练。

speaker: Unknown
en: You are.
zh: 你会的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'll just be honest.
zh: 我就诚实点。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not going to be fun.
zh: 这不会有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're not going to want to be there the whole time.
zh: 你不会想一直待在那里的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're going to feel embarrassed.
zh: 你会感到尴尬。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're going to hate watching them run plays with you on the sideline, and you're going to come home having survived.
zh: 你会讨厌看着他们奔跑做战术而你却在边线上，然后你会回到家，幸存了下来。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm going to be there for a hug.
zh: 我会在那里给你一个拥抱。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm going to let you know I'm proud of you.
zh: 我要让你知道我为你感到骄傲。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe I'm proudest of you that moment even more than when you scored the goal last game, because that's the hard stuff that's going to help you in life and we're going to get through it together."
zh: 也许我在那一刻最为你感到骄傲，甚至超过你在上一场比赛进球的时候，因为那些是生活中真正能帮到你的难题，我们要一起挺过去。"

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think for anyone listening, I find what's really powerful with anything is thinking, what end of the spectrum am I on?
zh: 所以我认为对于任何正在听的人来说，我发现对于任何事真正有效的方法是思考，我在光谱的哪一端？

speaker: Unknown
en: Because it always gives me insight into where I can grow.
zh: 因为它总是让我洞察到我可以成长的地方。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is the I believe you part easier for me or is the I believe in you part easier for me?
zh: 是“我相信你”这一部分对我来说更容易，还是“我相信你（潜力）”这一部分对我来说更容易？

speaker: Unknown
en: And there's no morality.
zh: 这其中没有道德问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just knowing where you are is helpful.
zh: 只要了解你在哪里是有帮助的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Do I like to say to people, "Come on, it's no big deal.
zh: 我喜欢对人们说，“来吧，这没什么大不了的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can do it."
zh: 你能行的。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, that's the I believe in you.
zh: 好的，那就是“我相信你（潜力）”。

speaker: Unknown
en: Ooh, I wonder if I'd be more effective if I focus on the first, I believe you.
zh: 噢，我想知道如果我专注于第一部分，“我相信你”，会不会更有效。

speaker: Unknown
en: Or do I lead as a parent, as a leader with, I believe you, but ooh, do I sometimes go into the hole with someone?
zh: 或者作为父母、作为领导者，我主要是用“我相信你”来引领，但是噢，我是不是有时会真的和某人一起陷进那个洞里？

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, okay.
zh: 噢，好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's the other part I'm going to play around with.
zh: 这是我要去摆弄的另一部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that gives us all something to action on.
zh: 我认为这给了我们所有人可以行动的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is such good advice.
zh: 这是如此好的建议。

speaker: Unknown
en: I got tingles as you were describing what you were telling your kid.
zh: 当你在描述你告诉孩子的话时，我起了鸡皮疙瘩。

speaker: Unknown
en: If there's anything you take away from this conversation, this is useful with kids, with partners, I think in work too, just this idea of I believe you and that makes sense.
zh: 如果你要从这次对话中带走什么，这对孩子、对伴侣都有用，我认为在工作中也是，就是“我相信你”和“这有道理”这个概念。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then somehow making it clear that I believe in you.
zh: 然后不知何故要明确表示“我相信你（潜力）”。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: See a more capable version of them than they can see in the moment.
zh: 看到一个比他们在当下能看到的更有能力的版本。

speaker: Unknown
en: Doing those two things at once, it's magic.
zh: 同时做这两件事，这简直是魔法。

speaker: Unknown
en: I talk a good game.
zh: 我说得好听。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's hard for me too.
zh: 这对我来说也很难。

speaker: Unknown
en: Again, these things are hard in practice, but at least, and I know you're like this too, it's helpful to have a framework to come back to.
zh: 再说一次，这些事情在实践中很难，但至少，我知道你也像这样，有一个可以回溯的框架是有帮助的。

speaker: Lenny
en: And just these words are s-
zh: 只是这些话语是——

speaker: Unknown
en: You're like this too.
zh: 你也像这样。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's helpful to have a framework to come back to.
zh: 有一个可以回溯的框架是有帮助的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just these words are so... And you don't have to say them exactly, but these specific ways of phrasing is actually really great.
zh: 只是这些话语是如此……而且你不需要确切地说出它们，但这些特定的措辞方式实际上真的很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's come back to how people can get your advice constantly.
zh: 让我们回到人们如何能持续获得你的建议。

speaker: Unknown
en: So a lot of people see your advice on TikTok and Instagram, just kind of on Twitter and all these places, just like, "Oh, there's Dr. Becky giving me some awesome advice."
zh: 所以很多人在 TikTok 和 Instagram 上看到你的建议，就像在 Twitter 上和所有这些地方，就像，“噢，Dr. Becky 正在给我一些很棒的建议。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Not a lot of people know you have an epic business and product and community.
zh: 没有多少人知道你有一个史诗般的商业、产品和社区。

speaker: Unknown
en: Talk about that, just what you've built there, what it is, what people should know.
zh: 谈谈那个，只是关于你在那里建立的东西，它是什么，人们应该知道什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: So what started as an... I put up my first Instagram post on, I think it was February 28th, 2020.
zh: 所以一开始是……我发布了我的第一条 Instagram 帖子，我想那是 2020 年 2 月 28 日。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it was two weeks before New York City at least shut down, probably a lot of places in the US.
zh: 那是纽约市关闭的两周前，可能美国很多地方也是如此。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I didn't have some premonition about COVID.
zh: 我对 COVID 没有任何预感。

speaker: Unknown
en: The timing was just kind of nutty.
zh: 时机就是有点疯狂。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I even put up that post without any business in mind.
zh: 我甚至发布那条帖子时没有任何商业想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: I felt so compelled by these ideas around what we've been talking about and how different they are from what we've been handed down to us as truth that the only way I can describe it is, this sounds like awful and visceral, but it felt like I was vomiting them out and I just needed somewhere to put them.
zh: 我对这些围绕我们一直在讨论的内容的想法感到如此迫切，以及它们与我们被传下来的真理有多么不同，我唯一能描述它的方式是，这听起来很糟糕和发自内脏，但感觉就像我在把它们吐出来，我只是需要一个地方安置它们。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so when I look back, I was like, "Why did I do that?"
zh: 所以当我回头看，我是想，“我为什么那样做？”

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know.
zh: 我不知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was more like relief than intentional goal setting.
zh: 这更像是一种解脱而不是有意的目标设定。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then New York City shut down.
zh: 然后纽约市关闭了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then what happened was things just took fire.
zh: 然后发生的事情是事情真的火了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what was really powerful and I just kind of watched happen and tried to respond to was the community that formed around it, what people wanted.
zh: 真正强大的，我只是看着它发生并试图回应的是围绕它形成的社区，以及人们想要什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: I started doing these live workshops just because I had so much more to say than I could put into a carousel post.
zh: 我开始做这些直播研讨会，只是因为我有太多话要说，多到我没法把它们都放进一个轮播帖子里。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the way people were connecting live, it still happens now in the live events.
zh: 而且人们在现场建立联系的方式，现在在直播活动中仍然发生着。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think certain ideas I have or stories I tell can be really impactful to people, but if I had to choose between that versus what happens in our community, I would bet on the community every time.
zh: 我认为我的某些想法或讲述的故事真的会对人们产生深远影响，但如果我不得不在那和我们的社区中发生的事情之间做选择，我每次都会把赌注押在社区上。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just amazing because I think these ideas tap into some value system or healing inside of ourselves and safe communities form around value systems.
zh: 这真是太神奇了，因为我认为这些想法触动了我们内心深处的某种价值观或疗愈机制，而安全的社区正是围绕着价值观形成的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so what ended up happening from there is people were just telling us.
zh: 所以从那里最终发生的事情是，人们只是在告诉我们。

speaker: Unknown
en: They actually kind of incepted this idea like, "Dr. Becky, I feel like you're doing parent school."
zh: 他们实际上有点把这种想法植入了我们的脑海，“Becky 博士，我觉得你在做家长学校。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I was like, "What?"
zh: 我当时说，“什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And they're like, "I go to school for everything that I've ever cared about."
zh: 他们就说，“我为了我关心的每一件事都去上学。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I always joke that a friend said, "Do you know how much money I spent on gardening school?
zh: 我总是开玩笑说有个朋友说过，“你知道我在园艺学校花了多少钱吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I like my flowers, but I care about my children more, but I've spent more time and I've invested more in my tiny little garden or my cooking or my hobby."
zh: 我喜欢我的花，但我更关心我的孩子，但我花在我在那小小的花园或者是我的烹饪或我的爱好的时间和精力更多。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Certainly I've spent more learning about Greek history, which for most of us, no shade to Greece, doesn't actually impact our life that much anymore than I have in parenting.
zh: 当然，我花在学习希腊历史上的时间肯定更多，这对我们大多数人来说，不是要黑希腊，但实际上对我们的生活影响不再那么大了，比我在育儿上投入的还要多。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I realized, and this is what this whole, our business is around, is I really feel like parenting is the last area in life that we glorify instinct alone, where we say, especially to women, this whole idea of maternal instinct, I don't know, this is probably a little controversial, I was like, "Wow, what a way to gaslight parents."
zh: 我意识到，这是我们要做的整个业务的核心理念，我真的觉得育儿是生活中我们最后一个仅仅靠美化本能来处理的领域，在这个领域我们会说，尤其是对女性，整个母性本能的想法，我不知道，这可能有点争议，我当时想，“哇，这真是煤气灯操纵家长的好方法。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Not to say there aren't some moments that are instinctual, but if I'm parenting by instinct, do you want to know what I'm going to do when my kid has a tantrum or when my kids lies in my face?
zh: 并不是说没有一些本能的时刻，但如果我靠本能育儿，你想知道当我的孩子发脾气或者当我的孩子当面撒谎时我会做什么吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not going to be pretty.
zh: 那场面不会好看。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know if any of us feel like that's a good option.
zh: 我不知道我们中是否有人觉得那是个好选择。

speaker: Lenny
en: I believe you. No, you would not do that.
zh: 我相信你。不，你不会那样做的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Right.
zh: 对。

speaker: Unknown
en: And instinct, by the way, what's instinct?
zh: 而且顺便说一下，什么是本能？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's everything we've learned until that point.
zh: 那是我们到那时为止学到的所有东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what feels naturally in parenting is simply how we were parented.
zh: 而在育儿中感觉自然的东西，仅仅是我们被养育的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's all that's going to be natural.
zh: 那将是唯一会感觉自然的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I really, like I started to get very angry with my community where like, why isn't there a better way?
zh: 所以我真的，我开始对我的社区感到非常生气，就像，为什么没有更好的方法？

speaker: Unknown
en: And by the way, with modern technology, shouldn't this be easy on your phone?
zh: 而且顺便说一下，有了现代技术，这不应该在你的手机上很容易实现吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Shouldn't you get access, not to make a moment perfect, but we all spiral after a hard moment.
zh: 你不应该能够获得一些东西，不是为了把一个时刻变得完美，而是我们都会在艰难的时刻之后陷入情绪螺旋。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not the spiral that makes us have a bad week.
zh: 让我们度过糟糕的一周的并不是那个螺旋。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's the way we extend the spiral from how we talk about ourselves and the bad patterns.
zh: 而是我们通过谈论自己和坏习惯来延长螺旋的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: That last weeks.
zh: 那会持续几周。

speaker: Unknown
en: These are the things that add up to our and our kids' mental health.
zh: 这些就是累积起来影响我们和我们的孩子心理健康的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I did, I started to get angry thinking there should just be a better option for parents to learn without shame, without the expectation of perfection, to have something in their pocket, to combine it with modern technology, now AI, so many different things, and to preserve the human interactive element.
zh: 我确实，我开始感到愤怒，觉得应该有一个更好的选择让家长去学习，没有羞耻感，没有完美的期望，口袋里有点东西，把它与现代技术结合起来，现在是 AI，还有各种不同的东西，并且保留人类互动的元素。

speaker: Unknown
en: So yeah, so that's what Good Inside is.
zh: 所以是的，这就是 Good Inside 的由来。

speaker: Unknown
en: My co-founder and I are both psychologists.
zh: 我的联合创始人和我都是心理学家。

speaker: Unknown
en: We've known each other forever, and we have a whole team where, yes, there's the podcast and Instagram.
zh: 我们彼此认识很久了，我们要有一个完整的团队，是的，有播客和 Instagram。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I always consider those flags, like a strategy and a tip we hear, they're like flags.
zh: 我总是把这些看作旗帜，就像我们听到的一个策略和技巧，它们就像旗帜。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're like things we try, but most of our struggle in parenting is we don't have a framework and an actual approach, which is the ground we would plant a flag into, which is why parenting feels like whack-a-mole where we're just trying things, right?
zh: 它们就像我们要尝试的东西，但我们大部分的育儿挣扎在于我们没有框架和实际的方法，那才是我们可以插旗的地面，这就是为什么育儿感觉像打地鼠，我们在那里只是尝试各种东西，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Even though we would never do that at work.
zh: 即使我们在工作中绝不会那样做。

speaker: Unknown
en: We don't even do that with gardening or tennis.
zh: 我们在园艺或网球中甚至都不那样做。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so Good Inside and our app to me is the most modern, alive, personalized, technologically informed way of feeling like I have something in my pocket and someone and a community too in my pocket that can just close the gap between how I kind of want to show up and how I do show up.
zh: 所以 Good Inside 和我们的应用程序对我来说是最现代、最鲜活、最个性化、最具技术含量的方式，感觉就像我口袋里有点东西，口袋里也有一个人和一个社区，它们可以缩小我想要表现出的样子和我实际表现出的样子之间的差距。

speaker: Unknown
en: None of us close the gap completely, but I do believe we deserve access to tools that can help us do that.
zh: 我们没有人能完全消除这个差距，但我确实相信我们应该获得能帮助我们要这样做的工具。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's what Good Inside as a business is built around.
zh: 这就是 Good Inside 作为一家企业围绕建立的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just to give you a more chance to plug what this is, exactly, what do people get when they say download the app, sign up for the community?

zh: 我认为我们触及了人性的各个层面的话题。

speaker: Unknown
en: Before we get to our very exciting lightning round, is there anything else that you wanted to share?
zh: 在我们进入非常激动人心的闪电轮之前，还有什么你想分享的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Anything else that you want to double down on?
zh: 还有什么你想加倍强调的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Kind of I think before we started recording, I was saying how excited I am.
zh: 我想在我们开始录音之前，我就在说我是多么兴奋。

speaker: Unknown
en: I really mean it.
zh: 我是真心的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're like a major celebrity to me and my whole team knows it, that of all of the podcasts I've been on, they're like, "You must be nervous for Lenny."
zh: 你对我来说就像一位大名人，我的整个团队都知道，在我上过的所有播客中，他们说，“你肯定会为 Lenny 紧张的。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I was like, "I am."
zh: 我说，“我是。”

speaker: Unknown
en: What I said to you is that I love your podcast because I just learn so much.
zh: 我对你说的是我喜欢你的播客，因为我学到了很多东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Right.
zh: 对。

speaker: Unknown
en: As a psychologist, CEO, obviously there's a lot for me to learn and I've learned so much from you and I really am addicted to learning.
zh: 作为心理学家、CEO，显然有很多东西要学，我从你那里学到了很多，我确实沉迷于学习。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the reason that's coming up for me now is I think about all the people who listen to your show who I think they're like that too.
zh: 现在这之所以浮现在我的脑海中，是因为我想到了所有听你的节目的人，我觉得他们也是那样的。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're forever learners.
zh: 他们是终身学习者。

speaker: Unknown
en: They love to learn.
zh: 他们热爱学习。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's why they love your podcast because you get so concrete and you follow the thread down the rabbit hole and it helps us feel more capable at work.
zh: 这就是他们喜欢你的播客的原因，因为你讲得非常具体，并且顺着线索深入探索，这帮助我们在工作中感到更有能力。

speaker: Unknown
en: And one of the things I've found interesting is so many people who have that forever learner growth mindset, whatever you want to call it at work, we forget to access it with our kids.
zh: 我发现有趣的一件事是，许多人在工作中拥有那种终身学习者成长型思维，无论你想怎么称呼它，我们在面对孩子时却忘记运用它。

speaker: Unknown
en: We say things like, "It is the way it is."
zh: 我们会说类似“它就是这样的”之类的话。

speaker: Unknown
en: I feel like people who listen to your podcast probably don't say that at work.
zh: 我觉得听你播客的人可能不会在工作中说那种话。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's why they're listening to their podcast.
zh: 这就是为什么他们在听他们的播客。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe it doesn't have to be the way it is.
zh: 也许事情不必非得是现在这样。

speaker: Unknown
en: Or, "Oh, I didn't know that before, but now I do.
zh: 或者，“噢，我以前不知道那个，但现在我知道了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I can try something new."
zh: 所以我可以尝试一些新东西。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And again, I'm never going to be perfect.
zh: 而且再说一遍，我永远不会完美。

speaker: Unknown
en: Perfect is creepy, but I can grow.
zh: 完美是很诡异的，但我可以成长。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm more addicted to growing and learning than being right.
zh: 我对成长和学习的上瘾胜过正确。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think, I guess I would just ask anyone listening, especially as a parent, to say, "What if I just took that mentality home?"
zh: 我想，我想我只是请任何在听的人，特别是作为父母，说，“如果我把这种心态带回家会怎样？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Like, okay, yelled at my kid.
zh: 比如，好吧，我冲我的孩子大吼大叫了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I didn't mess them up forever.
zh: 我并没有永远毁了他们。

speaker: Unknown
en: Do I know how to repair?
zh: 我知道怎么修复关系吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: By the way, did I have a parent who actually repaired with me?
zh: 顺便说一句，我有没有一个真的曾和我修复关系的父母？

speaker: Unknown
en: Because if I didn't, no wonder it feels awkward.
zh: 因为如果我没有，难怪这感觉很尴尬。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm kind of the first person in my whole lineage to give a true apology to my three-year-old.
zh: 我大概是我整个家族中第一个向我的三岁孩子真诚道歉的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Of course, that's going to feel hard.
zh: 当然，那会感觉很难。

speaker: Unknown
en: Or, yeah, I don't understand tantrums or I don't really understand why my good kid is lying to me, but maybe I could learn.
zh: 或者，是的，我不理解发脾气，或者我真的不理解为什么我的好孩子对我撒谎，但也许我可以学习。

speaker: Unknown
en: I learn things at work all the time and I give myself credit.
zh: 我一直在工作中学习东西，并且我也认可自己。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what if I took that way of being and just applied it to this other area?
zh: 如果我采用这种存在方式，并将其应用到这个其他领域会怎样？

speaker: Unknown
en: I'd both, I think, be more compassionate with myself, but I probably also feel better over time about how I was showing up.
zh: 我想我会对自己更富有同情心，但我也可能随着时间对我如何表现感觉更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I just think we forget to apply that because again, parenting has been told as something that should just feel natural when it doesn't, right?
zh: 我只是认为我们忘记应用这一点，因为同样，育儿被描述为某种应该感觉自然的事情，当它并不自然时，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: So if we think of it as a set of skills, it's actually very hopeful.
zh: 所以如果我们把它看作是一套技能，它实际上是很有希望的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, I learn skills from Lenny all the time.
zh: 噢，我一直从 Lenny 那里学到技能。

speaker: Unknown
en: I can do that in parenting too.
zh: 我也可以在育儿中那样做。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's such a beautiful message.
zh: 这是一个非常美好的信息。

speaker: Unknown
en: What I feel is anytime I watch one of your clips and then do that thing and then it works, it's like, oh, that expands your mind.
zh: 我感觉是每当我看你的一个片段，然后做那件事，然后它奏效了，就像，噢，那扩展了你的思维。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh wow, there's so much more I could learn.
zh: 哇，还有这么多我可以学习的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I should go deeper on what the hell I'm doing.
zh: 我应该更深入地研究我在到底做什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know what I'm doing.
zh: 我不知道我在做什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that is a really important message of like you can learn this skill.
zh: 所以这是一个非常重要的信息，就像你可以学习这项技能。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not something anyone just knows how to do.
zh: 这不是任何人都天生知道怎么做的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I like giving people very concrete things.
zh: 我喜欢给人们非常具体的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: So a question recently that I've asked my kids that have led to amazing moments just as a, is some version of, "If I could do one thing different this week to be a better parent to you, what would it be?"
zh: 所以最近我问我的孩子们的一个问题，作为……已经带来了惊人的时刻，是某种版本的，“如果我本周可以做一件不同的事来成为你们更好的父母，那会是什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: I guess it's kind of like a 360 degree-
zh: 我想这有点像是一个 360 度的——

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's [inaudible 01:25:38].

speaker: Unknown
en: Review.
zh: 复盘。

speaker: Unknown
en: Right.
zh: 对。

speaker: Unknown
en: And-
zh: 而且——

speaker: Unknown
en: Give me a raise.
zh: 给我加薪。

speaker: Unknown
en: Sometimes your kid says the hurtful things.
zh: 有时你的孩子会说伤人的话。

speaker: Unknown
en: "You can not be on your phone as much," which just get ready for it and just get ready to receive.
zh: “你可以不要那么多地看手机，”所以只需为此做好准备，并准备好去接收。

speaker: Unknown
en: "Okay.
zh: “好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Thank you for telling me that."
zh: 谢谢你告诉我那件事。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think the thing that stops us is we think our kid is going to say "stupid things."
zh: 我认为阻止我们的事情是我们认为我们的孩子会说“傻事”。

speaker: Unknown
en: "You could let me watch TV."
zh: “你可以让我看电视。”

speaker: Unknown
en: The truth is no matter what your kid says, it's a window into their world.
zh: 真相是不管你的孩子说什么，这都是了解他们世界的一个窗口。

speaker: Unknown
en: And again, you can be the pilot who listens but doesn't open the cockpit door.
zh: 同样，你可以做那个倾听但不打开驾驶舱门的飞行员。

speaker: Unknown
en: "Oh, what would it be like to watch all the TV you want?
zh: “噢，想看所有你想看的电视会是什么样？

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, your friends watch more.
zh: 噢，你的朋友们看得更多。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm so glad you told me that.
zh: 我很高兴你告诉我那件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, does that mean I can watch TV?
zh: 噢，那是不是意味着我可以看电视？

speaker: Unknown
en: Sweetie, that's not going to be one of the things I budge on, but maybe here's something we could do instead."
zh: 亲爱的，那不会是我在这件事上让步的事情之一，但也许这里有一些我们可以代替做的事情。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But just asking your kid that question and not expecting a profound answer.
zh: 但只要问你的孩子那个问题，而不期待一个深刻的回答。

speaker: Unknown
en: It makes me think if anyone thought, "What if my manager asked me that question?
zh: 这让我思考，有没有人想过，“如果我的经理问我那个问题会怎样？

speaker: Unknown
en: Wouldn't I immediately just feel better about my manager anyway just from the asking of the question?"
zh: 难道我不会仅仅因为这个问题就对我的经理感觉更好一点吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think it's just something so actionable that every time I do it with my kids, I notice their behavior's kind of better for the rest of the week just from that.
zh: 我认为这是如此可执行的事情，每次我和我的孩子这样做时，我注意到他们在这一周剩下的时间里行为都会因此变得好一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I want to give something high impact to anybody who's made it this far in the episode.
zh: 我想为任何听到节目这一部分的人提供一些高影响力的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love that that's like the reverse of this approach to this episode is like from the work environment, what can you bring to your kids?
zh: 我喜欢那是这种方法在本期节目中的反面，就像是从工作环境中，你能给你的孩子带来什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's like, do a little 360, ask for what feedback can you give me in my performance review as a parent this week.
zh: 就像是，做一个小的 360 度评估，询问在这个周作为我的表现反馈你能给我什么。

speaker: Dr. Becky Kennedy
en: Exactly.
zh: 确实如此。

speaker: Unknown
en: So good.
zh: 太好了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: With that, Dr. Becky, we reached our very exciting lightning round.
zh: 有了这个，Becky 博士，我们到了我们非常令人兴奋的闪电轮环节。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've got five questions for you.
zh: 我有五个问题要问你。

speaker: Unknown
en: Are you ready?
zh: 你准备好了吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Here we go.
zh: 我们开始吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: What are two or three books that you find yourself recommending most to other people?
zh: 哪两三本书是你发现自己最经常推荐给别人的？

speaker: Unknown
en: I love The Power of Moments.
zh: 我喜欢《时刻的力量》。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love Messy Middle and I got a lot from Creativity, Inc as well.
zh: 我喜欢《混乱的中途》，我也从《创意公司》那里学到了很多。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is there a recent movie or TV show you have really enjoyed?
zh: 有最近的一部电影或电视节目是你真的很喜欢的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I really liked Secrets We Keep, which was actually recommended to me by our product leader on Netflix.
zh: 我真的很喜欢《我们要保守的秘密》，这实际上是我们的 Netflix 产品负责人推荐给我的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I really loved KPop Demon Hunters so much.
zh: 我也非常喜欢《KPop 恶魔猎人》。

speaker: Unknown
en: I wrote a whole guide on it because I think it's just such an opener for really good, deep connected moments with our kids.
zh: 我写了一整篇关于它的指南，因为我认为它是开启与我们的孩子之间真正美好、深层连接时刻的绝佳契机。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so those two come to mind.
zh: 所以这两个浮现在脑海中。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is there a product you recently discovered that you really like?
zh: 有没有你最近发现的你真的很喜欢的产品？

speaker: Unknown
en: Could be an app, could be clothing, could be a gadget.
zh: 可以是一个应用程序，可以是衣服，也可以是一个小玩意。

speaker: Dr. Becky Kennedy
en: I started doing Liberty Puzzles. Do you know it? So some people-
zh: 我开始做 Liberty Puzzles。你知道它吗？所以有些人——

speaker: Unknown
en: No.
zh: 不知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: Have heard of Stave.
zh: 听说过 Stave。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're like these, but this is Liberty.
zh: 它们就像这些，但这是 Liberty。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're thick wooden puzzles where they're kind of hand cut pieces.
zh: 它们是那种厚木制拼图，其中的拼图块是手工切割的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You never know what's an edge.
zh: 你永远不知道哪一个是边缘。

speaker: Unknown
en: Sometimes the colors that connect are kind of the end of one color and start of another, so they're tricky.
zh: 有时连接的颜色是一种颜色的结束和另一种颜色的开始，所以它们很棘手。

speaker: Unknown
en: But having this out at night, number one, I find puzzling is something where you have to monotask.
zh: 但是晚上把它放在外面，第一，我发现拼图是一件你必须单调任务的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: You truly cannot multitask.
zh: 你真的不能多任务处理。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I find it so hard for me to monotask these days, even though it gives me such grounded energy.
zh: 我发现我最近很难做单调任务，尽管它给了我如此扎根的能量。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that allows that.
zh: 所以那允许了那样。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's something, yes, with my kids, but oddly enough, me and my husband will find ourselves doing it together at night versus being on the couch, doing work.
zh: 而且这件事，是的，和我的孩子一起，但奇怪的是，我和我的丈夫会在晚上发现自己一起做它，而不是坐在沙发上，做工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I feel like we're in the same world working on something together.
zh: 我感觉好像我们在同一个世界里一起做某事。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's been really fun for that as well.
zh: 这在那方面也真的很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: My kid has been really into puzzles recently.
zh: 我的孩子最近真的很喜欢拼图。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have this menorah puzzle that we got and he's just every bedtime, he wants to do it four times, finishes it, again.
zh: 我们有一个我们得到的烛台拼图，他每次睡前，他都想要做四次，完成了它，再一次。

speaker: Unknown
en: He speaks Spanish because his grandma speaks Spanish to him, so he's like mas, una mas.
zh: 他讲西班牙语，因为他奶奶对他讲西班牙语，所以他像是“mas, una mas”。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, I love it.
zh: 噢，我喜欢那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: Love puzzles.
zh: 热爱拼图。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I love that he's just like, "Break it apart.
zh: 我喜欢他就像，“把它拆开。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, let's start from the beginning."
zh: 好的，让我们从头开始。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Amazing.
zh: 令人惊叹。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Puzzles.
zh: 谜题。

speaker: Unknown
en: How fun.
zh: 多么有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Two more questions.
zh: 还有两个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you have a favorite life motto that you find useful in work or in life?
zh: 你有一句最喜欢的人生座右铭,是你发现在工作或生活中有用的吗?

speaker: Unknown
en: This feels hard because it is hard, not because I'm doing something wrong.
zh: 这感觉很难,是因为它本来就难,而不是因为我做错了什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just think this is so true in parenting at work.
zh: 我只是觉得这在育儿和工作中都是如此真实。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's so interesting how often something is hard and we tell ourselves the story that we're stupid or we're not good at it.
zh: 这太有意思了,某件事很难,我们经常告诉自己,说我们很笨或者我们不擅长这件事,这种情况发生的频率是如此之高。

speaker: Unknown
en: And there's something so simple about reminding myself, no, this feels hard because it is hard.
zh: 提醒自己这一点是如此简单:不,这感觉很难,是因为它本来就难。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm feeling this moment right.
zh: 我感觉对了当下的这个时刻。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just hard.
zh: 它就是很难。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I find that I always feel my feet on the ground again when I tell myself that.
zh: 我发现当我告诉自己这一点时,我总能重新感到脚踏实地。

speaker: Unknown
en: Great segue to our final question.
zh: 很好地过渡到了我们的最后一个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you're building a product, building a company.
zh: 所以你在构建一个产品,构建一家公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: As a non-product person, what's something that surprised you about the experience of building a product and building a company?
zh: 作为一个非产品领域的人,在构建产品和构建公司的体验中,有什么事情让你感到惊讶?

speaker: Unknown
en: It's funny.
zh: 这很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm hesitating only because I'm thinking of something and I'll say it, but I'm like, why did that surprise me?
zh: 我之所以犹豫,只是因为我在想某件事,我会说出来的,但我就像,为什么要那件事让我感到惊讶?

speaker: Unknown
en: It should be so obvious.
zh: 它应该是如此显而易见。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I guess it's true that a company is just people.
zh: 但我想这确实是真的,一家公司就是人。

speaker: Unknown
en: And before I started this company, I was in private practice, seeing people for therapy.
zh: 在我创办这家公司之前,我开私人诊所,为人们做心理治疗。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I feel like those skills are remarkably useful as the leader of a company where there's so many things I can't do.
zh: 我觉得那些技能作为一家公司的领导者是出奇有用的,因为有很多事情是我做不到的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're not going to ask me to financially model or data analytics, but I feel like there's really strong people who can do that.
zh: 你不会要求我做金融建模或数据分析,但我确实有很强的人能做到这些。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I believe that understanding people or just having the skill of attempting to understand people is a surprisingly, but I guess also retrospectively obvious, really good leadership skill.
zh: 但我相信,理解人,或者仅仅是拥有尝试理解人的技巧,是一项出奇地好——但我猜想回想起来也是显而易见的——领导技能。

speaker: Unknown
en: Dr. Becky, this was incredible.
zh: Dr. Becky,这真是太棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's such an honor to have you on.
zh: 很荣幸能邀请到你。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just see you everywhere all the time, have learned so much from you.
zh: 我到处都能看到你,从你那里学到了很多。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what a treat to have you in this podcast.
zh: 能在这个播客上采访你真是一大乐事。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have people are like, "What the hell is going on here?"
zh: 我有人会说,「这里到底发生了什么?」

speaker: Unknown
en: I learned a ton myself and I know people will too.
zh: 我自己也学到了很多,我知道大家也会的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Two final questions.
zh: 最后两个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: Where can folks find you, Good Inside, your book, anything else you want to share?
zh: 大家可以在哪里找到你,Good Inside,你的书,或者你想分享的任何其他东西?

speaker: Unknown
en: And then just how can listeners be useful to you?
zh: 然后就是听众们怎样才能对你有所帮助?

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh yeah, just goodinside.com.
zh: 哦是的,只要去 goodinside.com。

speaker: Unknown
